docs.title: 🇬🇧 Corso di Inglese
docs.text:
Benvenuto nel Corso di Inglese.

Questo percorso è progettato per accompagnarti da un livello A1 (principiante) fino a un livello C2 (avanzato), con un approccio pratico, progressivo e orientato alla comunicazione reale.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo del corso
docs.text:
Alla fine del percorso sarai in grado di:

  • capire conversazioni quotidiane
  • parlare con una buona fluidità
  • scrivere email e testi chiari
  • esprimere opinioni e argomentazioni
  • usare l’inglese nella vita reale (viaggi, lavoro, studio)

docs.title: 🧭 Come è strutturato il corso
docs.text: Il corso è diviso in 6 livelli:

docs.title: 🟢 A1 – Beginner
docs.text: Impari le basi dell’inglese: presentarti, fare domande semplici, parlare della tua vita.

docs.title: 🔵 A2 – Elementary
docs.text: Inizi a parlare della tua routine, del passato e dei tuoi piani.

docs.title: 🟡 B1 – Intermediate
docs.text: Diventi autonomo: riesci a sostenere conversazioni e scrivere testi semplici.

docs.title: 🔴 B2 – Upper-Intermediate
docs.text: Sviluppi fluidità, precisione e capacità di esprimere opinioni complesse.

docs.title: 🟣 C1 – Advanced
docs.text: Porti il tuo inglese a un livello avanzato: comunichi con sicurezza, colleghi idee in modo naturale e usi la lingua con precisione e controllo.

docs.title: ⚫ C2 – Mastery
docs.text: Raggiungi la padronanza completa: pensi in inglese, ti esprimi in modo naturale in qualsiasi contesto e utilizzi la lingua come un vero strumento di comunicazione.

docs.title: 📚 Come usare questo corso
docs.text:
Per ottenere risultati reali:

  1. Segui le unità in ordine
  2. Ripeti ad alta voce
  3. Scrivi ogni giorno (anche poco)
  4. Non tradurre mentalmente
  5. Rivedi spesso le unità precedenti

👉 L’inglese si impara usando la lingua, non solo studiandola.

docs.title: 🧠 Metodo
docs.text:
Ogni unità include:

  • Grammar in context
  • Vocabulary essenziale
  • Listening / Reading
  • Speaking guidato
  • Writing
  • Pronunciation
  • Review finale

docs.title: 🚀 Inizia subito
docs.text:
👉 Vai alla sezione A1 e inizia dalla Unità 01.

“Consistency beats intensity.” (La costanza batte l’intensità)

docs.title: 🎯 Cos’è questo corso
docs.text:
Corso di Inglese è un percorso completo progettato per portarti da livello A1 a livello B2 in modo chiaro, pratico e strutturato.

Non è un semplice insieme di lezioni. È un sistema costruito per aiutarti a:

  • capire l’inglese
  • usarlo davvero
  • parlare con sicurezza

docs.title: 🧠 Filosofia del corso
docs.text:
Molti corsi si concentrano su:

  • grammatica teorica
  • esercizi passivi
  • memorizzazione

👉 questo corso è diverso

docs.title: Il nostro approccio:
docs.text: Elemento Come lo trattiamo Grammatica semplice, chiara, utile Vocabolario reale e contestuale Speaking centrale, ogni giorno Writing progressivo Ripetizione fondamentale

docs.title: 🔁 Metodo di apprendimento
docs.text:
Il corso si basa su un principio semplice:

👉 impari quando usi, non quando studi

docs.title: Formula del corso
docs.text: Input + Output + Ripetizione = Fluency

docs.title: 📊 Struttura del percorso
docs.text:
Il corso è diviso in 4 livelli:

Livello Obiettivo A1 basi dell’inglese A2 comunicazione semplice B1 conversazione autonoma B2 fluidità e naturalezza

docs.title: 📚 Cosa troverai
docs.text:
All’interno del corso:

  • 40 unità progressive
  • spiegazioni chiare
  • esempi reali
  • esercizi pratici
  • speaking guidato
  • test di verifica

docs.title: 🗣️ Focus sullo speaking
docs.text:
Questo corso dà priorità a ciò che conta davvero:

👉 parlare

Per questo:

  • ogni unità include speaking
  • impari a creare frasi
  • impari a esprimerti

docs.title: ⚠️ Cosa NON è questo corso
docs.text:
Questo corso NON è:

❌ solo teoria ❌ solo grammatica ❌ solo esercizi

È:

✅ pratica ✅ uso reale ✅ comunicazione

docs.title: 🚀 A chi è rivolto
docs.text:
Questo corso è per te se:

  • parti da zero
  • hai studiato ma non parli
  • vuoi migliorare davvero
  • vuoi usare l’inglese nella vita reale

docs.title: 🎯 Risultato finale
docs.text:
Seguendo il corso correttamente sarai in grado di:

  • sostenere conversazioni
  • capire inglese parlato
  • esprimere opinioni
  • scrivere testi chiari
  • usare l’inglese con sicurezza

docs.title: 💡 Come ottenere risultati
docs.text:
Il corso funziona se:

  • studi con costanza
  • fai gli esercizi
  • parli ogni giorno

docs.title: 🔑 Regola fondamentale
docs.text: 👉 non cercare la perfezione 👉 cerca l’uso reale

docs.title: 🌍 Inglese reale
docs.text:
L’obiettivo non è:

❌ conoscere tutte le regole

L’obiettivo è:

✅ usare l’inglese nella vita reale

docs.title: 🧩 Integrazione con il sito
docs.text:
Per ottenere il massimo:

  • usa la sezione Grammar per i dubbi
  • usa Vocabulary per espandere il lessico
  • usa Exercises per praticare
  • usa Tests per verificarti
  • usa Metodo per studiare meglio

docs.title: 🔄 Progressione reale
docs.text:
Se segui il corso:

👉 non solo migliori 👉 cambi modo di pensare in inglese

docs.title: 🎯 Messaggio finale
docs.text:
Imparare inglese non è:

  • veloce
  • automatico

Ma è:

  • possibile
  • strutturato
  • raggiungibile

docs.title: 🚀 Il vero obiettivo
docs.text:
Non è dire:

👉 “Ho finito il corso”

È dire:

👉 “Posso usare l’inglese”

You are not studying English. You are building a skill.

docs.title: 🟢 Livello A1 – Beginner
docs.text:
Benvenuto nel livello A1.

Qui costruirai le fondamenta dell’inglese.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo A1
docs.text:
Alla fine di questo livello sarai in grado di:

  • presentarti
  • fare domande semplici
  • parlare della tua vita quotidiana
  • capire frasi base
  • descrivere persone e oggetti

docs.title: 📚 Cosa imparerai
docs.text:
• verbo to be
  • present simple
  • numeri, date, orari
  • lessico di base (casa, lavoro, famiglia)
  • domande semplici
  • uso di can / can't

docs.title: ⚠️ Attenzione
docs.text:
A questo livello è normale:

  • fare errori
  • parlare lentamente
  • tradurre mentalmente

👉 È parte del processo.

docs.title: 💡 Consiglio
docs.text:
Parla ad alta voce fin dal primo giorno.

Anche frasi semplici come:

  • "My name is..."
  • "I am from..."
  • "I work in..."

docs.title: 📖 Unità
docs.text:
1. Greetings and Introductions
  2. To Be
  3. Personal Information
  4. Everyday Objects
  5. Present Simple
  6. Daily Routine
  7. There is / There are
  8. Places in Town
  9. Can / Can't
  10. A1 Review

👉 Inizia con Unità 01

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • salutare
  • presentarti
  • dire il tuo nome e la tua provenienza
  • fare domande base

docs.title: Il verbo "to be"
docs.text: Subject Verb I am You are He/She is We are They are

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am Marco
  • You are a student
  • She is from Italy

docs.title: Greetings
docs.text:
• Hello
  • Hi
  • Good morning
  • Good afternoon
  • Good evening

docs.title: Introductions
docs.text:
• My name is...
  • I am...
  • Nice to meet you

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Hello! B: Hi! A: My name is John. What’s your name? B: I’m Anna. Nice to meet you. A: Nice to meet you too.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti ad alta voce:

  • Hello, my name is ___
  • I am from ___
  • Nice to meet you

👉 Poi rispondi:

  • What’s your name?
  • Where are you from?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 3 frasi:

  1. My name is ______
  2. I am from ______
  3. I am a ______

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione a:

  • "Hello" → /həˈloʊ/
  • "Name" → /neɪm/

👉 Non dire: “ello” → la H si pronuncia

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ Marco
  2. She ___ from Spain
  3. My name ___ Anna

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Presentati ad alta voce (anche da solo):

  • Name
  • Country
  • Job / Student

Speak from day one. Even simple English is real English.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare correttamente il verbo to be
  • fare frasi affermative, negative e domande
  • dare informazioni semplici su persone
  • rispondere a domande base

docs.title: Forma affermativa
docs.text: Subject Verb I am You are He is She is It is We are They are

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am a student
  • You are my friend
  • She is from Italy
  • They are teachers

docs.title: Forma negativa
docs.text:
Struttura:

👉 Subject + verb + NOT

  • I am not (I’m not)
  • You are not (You aren’t)
  • He is not (He isn’t)

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am not a teacher
  • She is not from Spain
  • They are not at home

docs.title: Forma interrogativa
docs.text:
Struttura:

👉 Verb + subject

  • Am I...?
  • Are you...?
  • Is he...?

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Are you a student?
  • Is she Italian?
  • Are they friends?

docs.title: Risposte brevi
docs.text:
• Yes, I am / No, I’m not
  • Yes, he is / No, he isn’t
  • Yes, they are / No, they aren’t

docs.title: Jobs
docs.text:
• student
  • teacher
  • engineer
  • doctor
  • worker

docs.title: Countries
docs.text:
• Italy
  • Spain
  • France
  • Germany
  • USA

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Are you a student? B: Yes, I am. A: Are you from Italy? B: Yes, I am. What about you? A: I’m from Spain.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I am a student
  • I am from Italy
  • She is a teacher
  • They are friends

👉 Rispondi:

  • Are you a student?
  • Are you from Italy?
  • Are you a teacher?

👉 Ora parla di te:

  • I am ___
  • I am from ___
  • I am a ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

  1. I am ______
  2. I am from ______
  3. I am not ______
  4. She is ______
  5. They are ______

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Contrazioni molto importanti:

  • I am → I’m
  • You are → You’re
  • He is → He’s
  • She is → She’s
  • They are → They’re

👉 Parla in modo naturale:

❌ I am Marco ✅ I’m Marco

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have 25 years ✅ I am 25 years old

❌ I am agree ✅ I agree

👉 In inglese to be è usato in modo diverso rispetto all’italiano.

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. She ___ from Italy
  2. They ___ students
  3. I ___ not a teacher
  4. ___ you from Spain?
  5. ___ he a doctor?

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Fai una mini presentazione:

  • Name
  • Country
  • Job

👉 Esempio:

Hi, I’m Marco. I’m from Italy. I’m a student.

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice (consigliato)
docs.text:
Fai queste domande a qualcuno (o immagina le risposte):

  • Are you a student?
  • Where are you from?
  • Are you a teacher?

Practice simple sentences every day. Fluency starts with repetition.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • chiedere e dare informazioni personali
  • fare domande con what, where, how old
  • parlare di nome, età, lavoro e provenienza
  • sostenere una mini conversazione reale

docs.title: Domande con WH-words
docs.text:
Struttura:

👉 WH-word + verb + subject

docs.title: What (cosa)
docs.text:
• What is your name?
  • What is your job?

👉 Forma contratta:

  • What’s your name?
  • What’s your job?

docs.title: Where (dove)
docs.text:
• Where are you from?

👉 Attenzione:

❌ Where you are from ✅ Where are you from

docs.title: How old (età)
docs.text:
• How old are you?

👉 Risposta:

  • I am 25 years old
  • I’m 25

docs.title: Personal information
docs.text:
• name
  • surname / last name
  • age
  • job
  • country
  • city

docs.title: Jobs (review + nuovi)
docs.text:
• student
  • teacher
  • doctor
  • engineer
  • manager

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Hi! What’s your name? B: My name is Luca. A: Where are you from? B: I’m from Italy. A: How old are you? B: I’m 28. And you? A: I’m 30. I’m from Spain.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti ad alta voce:

  • What’s your name?
  • Where are you from?
  • How old are you?

👉 Rispondi:

  • My name is ___
  • I’m from ___
  • I’m ___ years old

👉 Ora fai un mini dialogo (anche da solo):

  • What’s your name?
  • Where are you from?
  • How old are you?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un breve testo (3–5 frasi):

Esempio:

My name is Marco. I am from Italy. I am 25 years old. I am a student.

👉 Ora scrivi il tuo.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione alle contrazioni:

  • What is → What’s
  • Where are → Where’re (più difficile, spesso ridotto nel parlato)

docs.title: Focus su "years old"
docs.text: • years → /jɪərz/ 👉 non dire: “iers”

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have 30 years ✅ I am 30 years old

❌ What is your name? (sempre lento e rigido) ✅ What’s your name? (naturale)

❌ Where you are from? ✅ Where are you from?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. What ___ your name?
  2. Where ___ you from?
  3. How old ___ you?
  4. I ___ 25 years old
  5. My name ___ Anna

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma in domanda:

  1. You are from Italy → __?
  2. You are 30 → __?
  3. Your name is Marco → __?

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula una conversazione reale:

👉 Parla per 30 secondi:

  • Name
  • Age
  • Country
  • Job

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Fai queste domande a 3 persone (o immagina):

  • What’s your name?
  • Where are you from?
  • How old are you?

Real English starts with real questions. Ask. Answer. Repeat.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • nominare oggetti comuni
  • usare this / that / these / those
  • distinguere tra singolare e plurale
  • fare domande su oggetti

docs.title: This / That (singolare)
docs.text:
👉 Per UN oggetto

  • This = questo (vicino)
  • That = quello (lontano)

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• This is a book
  • That is a car

docs.title: These / Those (plurale)
docs.text:
👉 Per PIÙ oggetti

  • These = questi
  • Those = quelli

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• These are books
  • Those are cars

docs.title: Schema chiaro
docs.text: Vicino Lontano This (sing.) That (sing.) These (pl.) Those (pl.)

docs.title: Oggetti comuni
docs.text:
• book
  • phone
  • computer
  • table
  • chair
  • bag
  • pen
  • notebook
  • door
  • window

docs.title: Singolare vs plurale
docs.text:
• book → books
  • chair → chairs
  • pen → pens

👉 Regola base: aggiungi -s

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What is this? B: It’s a phone.

A: And what are these? B: They are books.

A: Is that your bag? B: Yes, it is.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • This is a book
  • That is a chair
  • These are pens
  • Those are computers

👉 Guarda intorno a te e parla:

  • This is a ___
  • That is a ___
  • These are ___
  • Those are ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • What is this?
  • What are these?
  • Is this your phone?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

  1. This is a ______
  2. That is a ______
  3. These are ______
  4. Those are ______
  5. This is my ______

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione a:

  • this → /ðɪs/
  • these → /ðiːz/

👉 Suono TH (molto importante)

Non dire: dis ❌ Ma: this ✅

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ This are books ✅ These are books

❌ These is a book ✅ This is a book

❌ That are cars ✅ Those are cars

👉 Singolare e plurale devono essere coerenti

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. This ___ a book
  2. These ___ pens
  3. That ___ a chair
  4. Those ___ tables

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma al plurale:

  1. This is a book → ______
  2. That is a chair → ______
  3. This is a pen → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Guarda la tua stanza e descrivi:

  • 3 oggetti vicino a te
  • 3 oggetti lontani

👉 Esempio:

  • This is my phone
  • These are my books
  • That is a door

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Descrivi la tua scrivania:

  • 5 oggetti
  • usa this / these

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Chiedi (o immagina):

  • What is this?
  • What are those?
  • Is this yours?

English starts with simple things. Name what you see.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare della tua vita quotidiana
  • descrivere abitudini
  • usare il present simple in modo corretto
  • costruire frasi semplici ma reali

docs.title: Forma base
docs.text:
👉 Subject + verb

  • I work
  • You live
  • They study

docs.title: Terza persona (he / she / it)
docs.text:
👉 aggiungi -s

  • He works
  • She lives
  • It eats

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I work in an office
  • You live in Milan
  • He works in a hospital
  • She studies English

docs.title: Verbi comuni
docs.text:
• work
  • live
  • eat
  • study
  • like
  • drink
  • go
  • play

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• every day
  • in the morning
  • at night
  • on Monday

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What do you do? B: I work in an office.

A: Where do you live? B: I live in Milan.

A: What does she do? B: She works in a hospital.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I work
  • I live in Italy
  • I study English
  • I drink coffee

👉 Ora parla di te:

  • I work in ___
  • I live in ___
  • I study ___
  • I eat ___

👉 Parla di un’altra persona:

  • He works in ___
  • She lives in ___
  • He eats ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi su di te:

  1. I work ______
  2. I live ______
  3. I eat ______
  4. I drink ______
  5. I study ______

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione alla -s finale

  • works → /wɜːrks/
  • lives → /lɪvz/

👉 Non ignorare la -s

❌ He work ✅ He works

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ He work ✅ He works

❌ She live in Italy ✅ She lives in Italy

❌ I am work ✅ I work

👉 NON usare to be con verbi normali

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
Impara questo schema:

  • I work
  • You work
  • He works

👉 Questo pattern è la base di tutto l’inglese.

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. He ___ (work) in an office
  2. She ___ (live) in Italy
  3. I ___ (eat) pizza
  4. They ___ (study) English

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma:

  1. I work → He ______
  2. I live → She ______
  3. I eat → He ______

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Descrivi la tua giornata (3–5 frasi):

Esempio:

I wake up at 7. I drink coffee. I work in an office. I eat lunch at 1.

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What do you do?
  • Where do you live?
  • What do you eat every day?

Speak about your life. That’s real English.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • descrivere la tua giornata
  • parlare di abitudini quotidiane
  • usare espressioni di tempo
  • costruire frasi in sequenza (storytelling base)

docs.title: Present Simple + Routine
docs.text:
👉 usiamo il present simple per parlare di abitudini

  • I wake up at 7
  • I work every day
  • I eat lunch at 1

docs.title: Sequenza delle azioni
docs.text:
Per collegare le frasi:

  • then
  • after that
  • and
  • in the morning
  • in the evening

docs.title: Esempio completo
docs.text: I wake up at 7. I drink coffee. Then I go to work. I eat lunch at 1. In the evening, I relax.

docs.title: Verbi della routine
docs.text:
• wake up
  • get up
  • eat
  • drink
  • go
  • work
  • study
  • sleep
  • watch (TV)
  • relax

docs.title: Espressioni di tempo
docs.text:
• in the morning
  • in the afternoon
  • in the evening
  • at night
  • every day

docs.title: Orari
docs.text:
• at 7
  • at 8:30
  • at noon

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I wake up at 7. I get up and drink coffee. Then I go to work. I work from 9 to 5. I eat dinner at 8. In the evening, I watch TV. I sleep at 11.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I wake up at 7
  • I drink coffee
  • I go to work
  • I eat dinner

👉 Ora parla della tua giornata:

  • I wake up at ___
  • I eat ___
  • I go to ___
  • I work ___
  • I sleep at ___

👉 Usa sequenze:

  • Then I ___
  • After that I ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi la tua routine (5–8 frasi)

Esempio:

I wake up at 7. I drink coffee. Then I go to work. I work all day. I eat dinner at 8. I watch TV. I sleep at 11.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione a:

  • wake up → /weɪk ʌp/
  • get up → /ɡet ʌp/

👉 La t in “get up” è veloce → ge-dup (fluido)

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I go to work at the 7 ✅ I go to work at 7

❌ I make a shower ✅ I take a shower

❌ I go at home ✅ I go home

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
Impara questo schema:

  • I wake up → I eat → I go → I work → I relax → I sleep

👉 Questa sequenza è la base per parlare.

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ up at 7
  2. I ___ coffee
  3. I ___ to work
  4. I ___ dinner
  5. I ___ at 11

docs.title: 🔄 Ordina
docs.text:
Metti in ordine:

  • I go to work
  • I wake up
  • I eat dinner
  • I sleep

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 descrivi la tua giornata completa

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What time do you wake up?
  • What do you do in the morning?
  • What do you do in the evening?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • orari precisi
  • 2 sequenze (then / after that)

Fluency starts when sentences connect. Not just words — but sequences.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • descrivere una stanza o un luogo
  • dire cosa c’è (e cosa non c’è)
  • usare there is / there are correttamente
  • parlare in modo più naturale e descrittivo

docs.title: There is (singolare)
docs.text:
👉 per UNA cosa

  • There is a book
  • There is a chair

docs.title: There are (plurale)
docs.text:
👉 per PIÙ cose

  • There are two books
  • There are three chairs

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• There is a table in the room
  • There are two windows
  • There is a computer on the desk

docs.title: In a room
docs.text:
• table
  • chair
  • bed
  • desk
  • lamp
  • door
  • window

docs.title: Numeri (review)
docs.text:
• one
  • two
  • three
  • four
  • five

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

There is a table in the room. There is a chair. There are two books on the table. There is a lamp next to the bed. There are three windows.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • There is a table
  • There is a chair
  • There are two books
  • There are three windows

👉 Guarda la tua stanza:

  • There is a ___
  • There are ___

👉 Descrivi:

  • There is a desk
  • There are two chairs
  • There is a computer

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Descrivi una stanza (5 frasi):

Esempio:

There is a bed. There is a table. There are two chairs. There is a lamp. There is a window.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Forma naturale:

  • There is → There’s
  • There are → spesso veloce: there’re

👉 Usa:

  • There’s a book
  • There are two books

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ There are a book ✅ There is a book

❌ There is two chairs ✅ There are two chairs

❌ Is a table ✅ There is a table

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• There is + 1 cosa
  • There are + più cose

👉 semplice ma fondamentale

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. There ___ a table
  2. There ___ two chairs
  3. There ___ a lamp
  4. There ___ three books

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma:

  1. There is a chair → (plurale) ______
  2. There are two books → (singolare) ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Descrivi:

👉 la tua stanza 👉 il tuo ufficio 👉 la tua cucina

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 descrivi una stanza completa

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What is in your room?
  • Is there a TV?
  • Are there books?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • numeri
  • 5 oggetti diversi
  • almeno 2 frasi con there are

English becomes real when you describe your world.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare della tua città
  • nominare luoghi comuni
  • fare domande con Is there...? / Are there...?
  • capire e partecipare a mini conversazioni reali

docs.title: Domande con There is / There are
docs.text:
👉 Struttura:

  • Is there + singolare?
  • Are there + plurale?

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Is there a restaurant?
  • Is there a bank?
  • Are there shops?
  • Are there parks?

docs.title: Risposte
docs.text:
• Yes, there is / No, there isn’t
  • Yes, there are / No, there aren’t

docs.title: Esempi completi
docs.text:
• Is there a supermarket? → Yes, there is
  • Are there restaurants? → Yes, there are

docs.title: Luoghi in città
docs.text:
• restaurant
  • bar
  • café
  • supermarket
  • bank
  • school
  • hospital
  • park
  • shop
  • hotel

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• near
  • far
  • in the city
  • in my town

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Is there a restaurant near here? B: Yes, there is.

A: Are there shops? B: Yes, there are.

A: Is there a bank? B: No, there isn’t.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • Is there a restaurant?
  • Are there shops?
  • There is a park
  • There are two cafés

👉 Parla della tua città:

  • There is a ___
  • There are ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • Is there a ___?
  • Are there ___?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5–7 frasi sulla tua città:

Esempio:

There is a supermarket. There is a park. There are restaurants. There is a school. There are shops.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • restaurant → /ˈres.tə.rɒnt/
  • café → /kæˈfeɪ/

👉 Non pronunciare tutte le lettere come in italiano

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ Is there restaurants? ✅ Are there restaurants?

❌ There is many shops ✅ There are many shops

❌ Is there a shops? ✅ Are there shops?

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• Is there + 1 cosa?
  • Are there + più cose?

👉 risposta breve:

  • Yes, there is
  • Yes, there are

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. ___ there a bank?
  2. ___ there shops?
  3. There ___ a restaurant
  4. There ___ two parks

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma in domanda:

  1. There is a bank → ______?
  2. There are shops → ______?

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Descrivi:

👉 la tua città 👉 un posto che conosci

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula una situazione reale:

👉 Sei un turista

Fai 3 domande:

  • Is there a restaurant?
  • Is there a hotel?
  • Are there shops?

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Is there a park in your city?
  • Are there restaurants?
  • Is there a hospital?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 5 luoghi diversi
  • 2 domande
  • 2 risposte

English is useful when you can ask for what you need.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di abilità (skills)
  • dire cosa puoi o non puoi fare
  • fare domande con can
  • rispondere in modo naturale

docs.title: Forma base
docs.text:
👉 Subject + can + verb

  • I can swim
  • You can speak English
  • She can drive

docs.title: Forma negativa
docs.text:
👉 Subject + cannot / can't + verb

  • I cannot swim
  • I can’t drive
  • He can’t speak English

docs.title: Forma interrogativa
docs.text:
👉 Can + subject + verb?

  • Can you swim?
  • Can she drive?
  • Can they speak English?

docs.title: Risposte brevi
docs.text:
• Yes, I can / No, I can’t
  • Yes, she can / No, she can’t

docs.title: Abilità (skills)
docs.text:
• swim
  • drive
  • speak
  • read
  • write
  • cook
  • play (football, piano)

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• very well
  • a little
  • not very well

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Can you swim? B: Yes, I can.

A: Can you drive? B: No, I can’t.

A: Can you speak English? B: Yes, a little.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I can swim
  • I can speak English
  • I can cook

👉 Ora parla di te:

  • I can ___
  • I can’t ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • Can you swim?
  • Can you drive?
  • Can you cook?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 3 cose che puoi fare 👉 3 cose che non puoi fare

Esempio:

I can swim. I can cook. I can speak English. I can’t drive. I can’t play piano. I can’t dance.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • can → /kæn/
  • can’t → /kɑːnt/ (UK)

👉 importante la differenza:

  • can (debole) → kən
  • can’t (forte) → KANT

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I can to swim ✅ I can swim

❌ I can’t to drive ✅ I can’t drive

❌ Can you can swim? ✅ Can you swim?

👉 Dopo can → verbo base (senza “to”)

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I can + verbo
  • I can’t + verbo

👉 semplicissimo → molto potente

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ swim
  2. She ___ drive
  3. He ___ speak English
  4. Can you ___?

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma:

  1. I can swim → (negativo) ______
  2. She can drive → (negativo) ______
  3. You can cook → (domanda) ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 5 abilità tue 👉 3 cose che non sai fare

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Fai una mini intervista:

👉 (anche da solo)

  • Can you swim?
  • Can you cook?
  • Can you speak English?

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Can you speak English well?
  • Can you drive?
  • Can you cook?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • “very well”
  • “a little”

👉 esempio:

  • I can speak English a little
  • I can cook very well

Ability is language power. Say what you can do.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare tutte le strutture A1 insieme
  • parlare di te in modo continuo
  • fare domande e rispondere
  • descrivere oggetti e luoghi
  • esprimere abilità

docs.title: 🧠 Grammar Review
docs.text:
Hai studiato:

  • To be (I am, you are...)
  • Present Simple (I work, she works)
  • There is / There are
  • Can / Can’t
  • WH questions (what, where, how old)

👉 Ora li usiamo insieme.

docs.title: 📚 Vocabulary Review
docs.text:
Sai parlare di:

  • informazioni personali
  • routine
  • oggetti
  • città
  • abilità

👉 Ora devi combinarli.

docs.title: 📝 TEST 1 – Grammar
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ from Italy
  2. She ___ a teacher
  3. They ___ students
  4. He ___ (work) in an office
  5. There ___ a table
  6. There ___ two chairs
  7. I ___ swim
  8. She ___ drive
  9. ___ you from Spain?
  10. ___ there a bank?

docs.title: 📝 TEST 2 – Trasformazioni
docs.text:
1. I am a student → (negativo) ______
  2. She works → (negativo) ______
  3. There is a book → (plurale) ______
  4. You can swim → (domanda) ______
  5. You are from Italy → (domanda) ______

docs.title: 🗣️ SPEAKING TEST (molto importante)
docs.text:
👉 Parla per 1 minuto (senza fermarti)

Devi dire:

  • Name
  • Age
  • Country
  • Job
  • Daily routine
  • 3 abilities
  • Describe your room

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: Hi, my name is Marco. I am 25 years old. I am from Italy. I work in an office. I wake up at 7 and I drink coffee. I can cook and I can speak English a little. There is a table in my room and there are two chairs.

docs.title: ✍️ WRITING TEST
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (8–10 frasi):

Devi includere:

  • informazioni personali
  • routine
  • abilità
  • descrizione di un luogo

docs.title: 🧠 THINK & SPEAK
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What is your name?
  • Where are you from?
  • What do you do?
  • What do you do every day?
  • Can you cook?
  • Is there a park in your city?

docs.title: 🔊 PRONUNCIATION CHECK
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I’m from Italy
  • I work every day
  • There are two chairs
  • I can speak English

👉 fluido, senza pause

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (check finale)
docs.text:
❌ I have 25 years ✅ I am 25

❌ He work ✅ He works

❌ There is two chairs ✅ There are two chairs

❌ I can to swim ✅ I can swim

docs.title: 🚀 FINAL TASK (livello reale)
docs.text:
Simula una situazione reale:

👉 incontri una persona nuova

Parla per 1–2 minuti:

  • presentati
  • fai 2 domande
  • parla della tua vita
  • descrivi la tua città

docs.title: 🎯 Autovalutazione
docs.text:
Se riesci a:

✅ parlare per 1 minuto ✅ fare domande ✅ capire frasi semplici ✅ scrivere un testo breve

👉 sei A1 ✔️

docs.title: 💬 Next step
docs.text:
👉 Sei pronto per A2

Nel prossimo livello imparerai:

  • passato (past simple)
  • futuro
  • conversazioni più naturali

If you can speak, you are learning. If you can continue, you are improving.

docs.title: 🔵 Livello A2 – Elementary
docs.text: A questo livello inizi a usare l’inglese in modo più attivo.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo A2
docs.text:
• parlare della tua routine
  • descrivere il passato
  • fare piani per il futuro
  • capire conversazioni semplici

docs.title: 📚 Cosa imparerai
docs.text:
• present continuous
  • past simple
  • comparativi e superlativi
  • futuro (will / going to)
  • lessico quotidiano più ampio

docs.title: 💡 Consiglio
docs.text:
Non puntare alla perfezione. Punta a comunicare.

👉 Continua con Unità 11

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di azioni in corso (adesso)
  • descrivere cosa sta succedendo
  • usare il present continuous correttamente
  • distinguere tra “now” e “every day”

docs.title: Forma base
docs.text:
👉 Subject + to be + verb-ing

  • I am working
  • You are studying
  • She is eating

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am eating
  • You are working
  • He is sleeping
  • They are watching TV

docs.title: Forma negativa
docs.text:
• I am not working
  • She is not eating
  • They are not studying

👉 contrazioni:

  • I’m not
  • She isn’t
  • They aren’t

docs.title: Forma interrogativa
docs.text:
👉 To be + subject + verb-ing

  • Are you working?
  • Is she eating?
  • Are they watching TV?

docs.title: Verbi comuni
docs.text:
• eat
  • work
  • study
  • sleep
  • watch
  • read
  • talk

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• now
  • at the moment
  • right now

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What are you doing? B: I’m eating.

A: Are you working? B: No, I’m not. I’m relaxing.

A: What is she doing? B: She is studying.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I am working
  • I am eating
  • I am studying
  • I am watching TV

👉 Ora descrivi cosa fai adesso:

  • I am ___
  • I am ___ right now

👉 Fai domande:

  • What are you doing?
  • Are you working?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi su cosa stai facendo ORA:

Esempio:

I am sitting. I am reading. I am studying English. I am drinking coffee. I am listening.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • working → /ˈwɜːrkɪŋ/
  • eating → /ˈiːtɪŋ/

👉 finale -ing = /ɪŋ/ (non “ingh” italiano)

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I am work ✅ I am working

❌ I working ✅ I am working

❌ She is eat ✅ She is eating

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I am working
  • You are working
  • He is working

👉 struttura sempre uguale → cambia solo il verbo

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto base
docs.text:
👉 differenza chiave:

  • I work → routine
  • I am working → adesso

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I am ___ (eat)
  2. She is ___ (study)
  3. They are ___ (watch)
  4. Are you ___ (work)?

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma:

  1. I work → (now) ______
  2. She eats → (now) ______
  3. They study → (now) ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Descrivi:

👉 cosa stai facendo ora 👉 cosa fanno altre persone

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Guarda intorno a te:

👉 descrivi 3 persone

  • He is ___
  • She is ___
  • They are ___

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What are you doing now?
  • Are you studying?
  • Are you working?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 descrivi cosa succede intorno a te ORA

English becomes alive when you describe the present.

docs.title: Unità 12 – Present Simple vs Present Continuous
🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • distinguere tra routine e azioni in corso
  • usare correttamente present simple e present continuous
  • evitare errori molto comuni
  • parlare in modo più naturale

docs.title: Present Simple → abitudini
docs.text:
👉 cose che fai sempre / regolarmente

  • I work every day
  • I drink coffee in the morning
  • She lives in Italy

docs.title: Present Continuous → adesso
docs.text:
👉 cosa succede ORA

  • I am working
  • I am drinking coffee
  • She is living in Milan

docs.title: ⚖️ Differenza chiave
docs.text: Present Simple Present Continuous routine adesso always now every day at the moment

docs.title: 📚 Esempi chiari
docs.text:
• I work → lavoro (in generale)
  • I am working → sto lavorando ora
  • I eat pizza → mangio pizza (abitualmente)
  • I am eating pizza → sto mangiando pizza ora
  • She lives in Rome → vive a Roma
  • She is living in Rome → vive a Roma (temporaneamente)

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What do you do? B: I work in an office.

A: What are you doing now? B: I am working from home.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I work every day

  • I am working now

  • I study English

  • I am studying now

👉 Ora prova:

  • I ___ every day
  • I am ___ now

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 3 routine 👉 3 azioni adesso

Esempio:

I work every day. I drink coffee in the morning. I study English.

I am sitting. I am reading. I am learning English.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • working → /ˈwɜːrkɪŋ/
  • work → /wɜːrk/

👉 differenza tra: - forma base - forma in -ing

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I am working every day ✅ I work every day

❌ I work now ✅ I am working now

❌ She is work ✅ She is working

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern mentale (IMPORTANTISSIMO)
docs.text:
👉 chiediti sempre:

  • È una routine? → Present Simple
  • È adesso? → Present Continuous

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Scegli la forma corretta:

  1. I (work / am working) now
  2. I (work / am working) every day
  3. She (eats / is eating) now
  4. They (study / are studying) at the moment

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I work → (now) ______
  2. She eats → (now) ______
  3. I am working → (routine) ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa fai ogni giorno 👉 cosa stai facendo ora

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:

Understanding time is understanding English.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare del passato
  • raccontare cosa hai fatto
  • usare il past simple (verbi regolari e irregolari)
  • fare e rispondere a domande sul passato

docs.title: Forma base (verbi regolari)
docs.text:
👉 Subject + verb + -ed

  • I worked
  • I played
  • I watched

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I worked yesterday
  • I watched TV
  • I played football

docs.title: Verbi irregolari (molto importanti)
docs.text:
• go → went
  • eat → ate
  • drink → drank
  • see → saw

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I went to work
  • I ate pizza
  • I drank coffee
  • I saw a film

docs.title: Espressioni di tempo
docs.text:
• yesterday
  • last night
  • last week
  • last year
  • two days ago

docs.title: Verbi utili
docs.text:
• go
  • eat
  • drink
  • watch
  • see
  • play
  • work

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

Yesterday I woke up at 7. I drank coffee. I went to work. I worked all day. In the evening, I watched TV. I slept at 11.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I worked yesterday
  • I went to work
  • I ate pizza
  • I watched TV

👉 Ora parla:

  • Yesterday I ___
  • Last night I ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5–8 frasi:

👉 cosa hai fatto ieri

Esempio:

Yesterday I woke up at 7. I drank coffee. I went to work. I worked all day. I watched TV.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione alla -ed

  • worked → /t/
  • played → /d/
  • wanted → /ɪd/

👉 NON sempre si legge “ed”

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I go yesterday ✅ I went yesterday

❌ I goed ✅ I went

❌ I did went ✅ I went

👉 passato = forma unica (no doppio verbo)

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I work → I worked
  • I go → I went

👉 devi memorizzare i verbi irregolari

docs.title: ❓ Domande (introduzione)
docs.text:
👉 base:

  • What did you do yesterday?

👉 risposta:

  • I worked
  • I went out

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ (work) yesterday
  2. I ___ (go) to work
  3. I ___ (eat) pizza
  4. I ___ (watch) TV

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma al passato:

  1. I work → ______
  2. I eat → ______
  3. I go → ______
  4. I drink → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa hai fatto ieri 👉 cosa hai fatto la scorsa settimana

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 racconta la tua giornata di ieri

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What did you do yesterday?
  • Did you work yesterday?
  • Did you watch TV?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 5 verbi diversi
  • 2 espressioni di tempo

The past is where stories begin.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di viaggi e vacanze
  • raccontare esperienze passate
  • usare il past simple in modo più naturale
  • sostenere una conversazione reale

docs.title: Past Simple (review)
docs.text:
👉 per parlare del passato

  • I went to Rome
  • I stayed in a hotel
  • I visited a museum

docs.title: Sequenza (molto importante)
docs.text:
Per raccontare:

  • and
  • then
  • after that

docs.title: Esempio completo
docs.text: I went to Paris. I stayed in a hotel. I visited museums. Then I ate in a restaurant. After that, I walked in the city.

docs.title: Travel
docs.text:
• travel
  • trip
  • holiday
  • hotel
  • airport
  • train
  • plane

docs.title: Attività
docs.text:
• visit
  • stay
  • eat
  • go out
  • walk
  • see

docs.title: Luoghi
docs.text:
• city
  • beach
  • mountains
  • restaurant
  • museum

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

Last summer I went to Spain. I stayed in a hotel near the beach. I visited Barcelona. I ate in many restaurants. In the evening, I walked in the city.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I went to Spain
  • I stayed in a hotel
  • I visited a city
  • I ate in a restaurant

👉 Ora parla:

  • I went to ___
  • I stayed in ___
  • I visited ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6–8 frasi:

👉 descrivi una vacanza

Esempio:

I went to Rome. I stayed in a hotel. I visited museums. I ate pizza. I walked in the city.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • visited → /ˈvɪzɪtɪd/
  • stayed → /steɪd/
  • walked → /wɔːkt/

👉 finale -ed cambia suono

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have been in Rome (A2 early → meglio evitare) ✅ I went to Rome

❌ I go to Spain last year ✅ I went to Spain last year

❌ I stayed in hotel ✅ I stayed in a hotel

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I went to ___
  • I stayed in ___
  • I visited ___
  • I ate ___

👉 questo pattern = conversazione reale

docs.title: ❓ Domande utili
docs.text:
• Where did you go?
  • Where did you stay?
  • What did you do?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ (go) to Italy
  2. I ___ (stay) in a hotel
  3. I ___ (visit) a museum
  4. I ___ (eat) pizza

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I go → ______
  2. I stay → ______
  3. I visit → ______
  4. I eat → ______

docs.text:
Parla:

👉 un viaggio che hai fatto 👉 un posto che ti è piaciuto

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 racconta una vacanza

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Where did you go on holiday?
  • What did you do?
  • Did you stay in a hotel?

docs.text:

Travel stories are the easiest way to speak English.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • fare confronti tra due cose
  • dire cosa è “più” o “meno”
  • esprimere preferenze
  • usare comparativi e superlativi

docs.title: Comparatives (confronto tra 2)
docs.text:
👉 struttura:

  • adjective + -er + than

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Rome is bigger than Milan
  • This book is cheaper than that one
  • English is easier than German

docs.title: Aggettivi lunghi
docs.text:
👉 usa more

  • more expensive
  • more interesting
  • more difficult

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• This hotel is more expensive
  • This city is more interesting

docs.title: 🏆 Superlatives (il massimo)
docs.text:
👉 struttura:

  • the + adjective + -est

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Rome is the biggest city
  • This is the cheapest hotel

docs.title: Aggettivi lunghi
docs.text:
👉 usa the most

  • the most expensive
  • the most interesting

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• This is the most beautiful place
  • It is the most expensive restaurant

docs.title: Aggettivi comuni
docs.text:
• big
  • small
  • fast
  • slow
  • easy
  • difficult
  • expensive
  • cheap
  • beautiful

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

Rome is bigger than Milan. It is also more beautiful. This hotel is cheaper than that one. This is the best restaurant in the city.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • This is bigger
  • This is smaller
  • This is more expensive
  • This is cheaper

👉 Confronta:

  • A is bigger than B
  • This is cheaper than that

👉 Superlativi:

  • This is the best
  • This is the biggest

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 confronta due cose

Esempio:

Rome is bigger than Milan. Pizza is better than pasta. This phone is more expensive.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • bigger → /ˈbɪɡər/
  • cheaper → /ˈtʃiːpər/

👉 non leggere tutto come italiano

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ more bigger ✅ bigger

❌ the more expensive ✅ the most expensive

❌ more cheap ✅ cheaper

👉 NON usare “more” con aggettivi corti

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• short adjective → bigger
  • long adjective → more interesting

docs.title: 🔁 Schema semplice
docs.text: Tipo Forma corto bigger lungo more interesting superlativo corto biggest superlativo lungo most interesting

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. This is ___ (big)
  2. This is ___ than that (cheap)
  3. This is ___ (expensive)
  4. This is the ___ (good)

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Trasforma:

  1. big → ______ (comparativo)
  2. small → ______
  3. expensive → ______
  4. interesting → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 confronta:

  • due città
  • due cibi
  • due posti

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 descrivi:

  • la tua città
  • la città migliore che conosci

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Which is better?
  • Which is cheaper?
  • What is the best place in your city?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 3 comparativi
  • 2 superlativi

Comparison creates real opinions. And opinions create real conversations.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • ordinare cibo
  • fare acquisti
  • parlare di quantità (base)
  • sostenere conversazioni semplici in negozio o ristorante

docs.title: Countable vs Uncountable (base)
docs.text:
👉 Countable (numerabili)

  • a pizza
  • two apples
  • three sandwiches

👉 Uncountable (non numerabili)

  • water
  • coffee
  • bread
  • rice

docs.title: Uso base
docs.text:
• I want a pizza
  • I want some water

docs.title: Food
docs.text:
• pizza
  • pasta
  • bread
  • rice
  • chicken
  • salad

docs.title: Drinks
docs.text:
• water
  • coffee
  • tea
  • juice

docs.title: Shopping
docs.text:
• shop
  • supermarket
  • buy
  • price
  • money

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue (restaurant):

A: What would you like? B: I would like a pizza and water.

A: Anything else? B: No, thank you.

Dialogue (shop):

A: Can I help you? B: Yes, I want apples.

A: How many? B: Two, please.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I want a pizza
  • I want some water
  • I buy food
  • I go to the supermarket

👉 Ora parla:

  • I want ___
  • I buy ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • Can I have ___?
  • How much is it?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 cosa mangi o compri

Esempio:

I buy bread. I drink coffee. I eat pizza. I want water. I buy fruit.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • water → /ˈwɔːtər/
  • coffee → /ˈkɒfi/

👉 NON leggere come italiano

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I want a water ✅ I want water

❌ I want breads ✅ I want bread

❌ I buy a milk ✅ I buy milk

👉 alcuni cibi NON si contano

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I want + food
  • I buy + food
  • I eat + food

👉 struttura semplice → molto usata

docs.title: ❓ Domande utili
docs.text:
• What would you like?
  • How much is it?
  • Can I have a coffee?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I want ___ pizza
  2. I drink ___ coffee
  3. I buy ___ bread
  4. I eat ___ chicken

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. water → (frase) I want ______
  2. pizza → I want ______
  3. coffee → I drink ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa mangi ogni giorno 👉 cosa compri

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula:

👉 sei al ristorante

  • I would like ___
  • I want ___

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What do you eat?
  • What do you drink?
  • What do you buy?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 5 cibi
  • 3 bevande
  • 1 dialogo

English is useful when you can order what you want.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di quantità in modo corretto
  • usare some e any
  • usare much e many
  • evitare errori molto comuni

docs.title: Some
docs.text:
👉 usato in frasi positive

  • I have some water
  • I want some apples

docs.title: Any
docs.text:
👉 usato in domande e negative

  • Do you have any water?
  • I don’t have any money

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have some coffee
  • I don’t have any coffee
  • Do you have any coffee?

docs.title: Many → countable (numerabili)
docs.text:
• many apples
  • many books

docs.title: Much → uncountable (non numerabili)
docs.text:
• much water
  • much money

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have many books
  • I don’t have much money

docs.title: Countable
docs.text:
• apples
  • books
  • chairs
  • shops

docs.title: Uncountable
docs.text:
• water
  • coffee
  • money
  • food

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Do you have any money? B: No, I don’t have any money.

A: Do you have any apples? B: Yes, I have some apples.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I have some water
  • I don’t have any money
  • Do you have any food?

👉 Ora prova:

  • I have some ___
  • I don’t have any ___
  • Do you have any ___?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

  1. I have some ______
  2. I don’t have any ______
  3. Do you have any ______?
  4. I have many ______
  5. I don’t have much ______

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • some → /sʌm/
  • any → /ˈeni/
  • many → /ˈmeni/

👉 suoni diversi → non confondere

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I don’t have some money ✅ I don’t have any money

❌ I have many money ✅ I have much money

❌ I have much apples ✅ I have many apples

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• positive → some
  • negative → any
  • question → any

docs.title: 🔁 Schema semplice
docs.text: Tipo Uso positive some negative any question any countable many uncountable much

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I have ___ water
  2. I don’t have ___ money
  3. Do you have ___ food?
  4. I have ___ apples
  5. I don’t have ___ time

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I have water → I have ___ water
  2. I don’t have money → I don’t have ___ money
  3. apples → I have ___ apples

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa hai 👉 cosa non hai

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula:

👉 sei al supermercato

  • I have some ___
  • I need some ___
  • I don’t have any ___

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Do you have any money?
  • Do you have any food?
  • How many apples do you have?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 3 frasi con some
  • 3 frasi con any
  • 2 con many
  • 2 con much

Natural English depends on small words like some and any.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare del futuro
  • fare piani
  • esprimere decisioni
  • usare will e going to correttamente

docs.title: Going to → piani
docs.text:
👉 qualcosa che hai già deciso

  • I am going to travel
  • I am going to study
  • I am going to eat pizza

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am going to visit Rome
  • I am going to work tomorrow
  • I am going to study English

docs.title: ⚡ Will → decisioni spontanee
docs.text:
👉 decisione presa ORA

  • I will help you
  • I will call you
  • I will go

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I will help you
  • I will open the door
  • I will call later

docs.title: ⚖️ Differenza chiave
docs.text: Going to Will piano decisione immediata già deciso deciso ora

docs.title: Esempio chiaro
docs.text:
• I am going to travel tomorrow (già deciso)
  • Ok, I will go with you (decido ora)

docs.title: Future expressions
docs.text:
• tomorrow
  • next week
  • tonight
  • later

docs.title: Verbi utili
docs.text:
• travel
  • study
  • work
  • call
  • help
  • go

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What are you going to do tomorrow? B: I am going to study.

A: I’m tired. B: I will help you.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I am going to study
  • I am going to travel
  • I will help
  • I will call

👉 Ora parla:

  • I am going to ___
  • I will ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • What are you going to do?
  • Will you help me?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 3 con going to 👉 3 con will

Esempio:

I am going to study tomorrow. I am going to travel next week. I am going to eat pizza.

I will help you. I will call you. I will go later.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Forma naturale:

  • going to → gonna (informale) 👉 I’m gonna go

  • will → spesso debole 👉 I’ll help

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I will to go ✅ I will go

❌ I going to study ✅ I am going to study

❌ I will going ✅ I will go

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I am going to + verbo
  • I will + verbo

👉 mai usare “to” dopo will

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ going to study
  2. I will ___ (go)
  3. She is going to ___ (work)
  4. I will ___ you

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I study tomorrow → ______
  2. I help you → ______
  3. I travel next week → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa farai domani 👉 cosa farai nel weekend

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1 minuto:

👉 i tuoi piani per il futuro

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What are you going to do tomorrow?
  • Will you study?
  • Will you travel?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 3 piani
  • 2 decisioni spontanee

The future starts when you can talk about it.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di esperienze di vita
  • fare domande con Have you ever...?
  • rispondere in modo naturale
  • sostenere una conversazione reale

docs.title: Struttura base
docs.text:
👉 Have + subject + past participle

  • Have you ever been to Rome?
  • Have you ever eaten sushi?

docs.title: Risposte
docs.text:
• Yes, I have
  • No, I haven’t

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have been to Spain
  • I have eaten pizza
  • I have seen that film

docs.title: Esperienze
docs.text:
• travel
  • visit
  • eat
  • see
  • try
  • go

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• ever
  • never

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have never been to London
  • I have never tried sushi

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Have you ever been to Paris? B: Yes, I have.

A: Have you ever eaten sushi? B: No, I haven’t.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • Have you ever been to Italy?
  • Have you ever eaten sushi?

👉 Ora prova:

  • Have you ever ___?

👉 Rispondi:

  • Yes, I have
  • No, I haven’t

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 3 esperienze 👉 2 cose mai fatte

Esempio:

I have been to Rome. I have eaten pizza. I have seen a film. I have never tried sushi. I have never been to London.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • have → spesso debole: /həv/
  • ever → /ˈevər/

👉 “Have you ever” → suona fluido: hav-you-ever

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ Have you ever went ✅ Have you ever gone

❌ I have see ✅ I have seen

❌ I have been in Rome ✅ I have been to Rome

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• Have you ever + verbo?
  • I have + verbo

👉 struttura sempre uguale

docs.title: 🔁 Differenza con Past Simple
docs.text:
👉 molto importante:

  • Past Simple → quando (when)
  • Present Perfect → esperienza (life)

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text:
• I went to Rome last year (quando)
  • I have been to Rome (esperienza)

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. Have you ever ___ (eat) sushi?
  2. I have ___ (be) to Italy
  3. I have never ___ (try) that
  4. Have you ever ___ (see) that film?

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I went to Rome → I have ______
  2. I ate sushi → I have ______
  3. I saw that film → I have ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 3 esperienze 👉 3 cose mai fatte

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Fai una mini intervista:

👉 (anche da solo)

  • Have you ever travelled?
  • Have you ever eaten sushi?
  • Have you ever been to another country?

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Have you ever been abroad?
  • Have you ever tried sushi?
  • Have you ever seen a famous place?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • 5 domande
  • 5 risposte
  • usa never

Conversations start with experiences.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare tutti i tempi base (present, past, future)
  • sostenere una conversazione semplice ma reale
  • parlare di esperienze, piani e abitudini
  • combinare grammatica e vocabolario

docs.title: 🧠 Grammar Review
docs.text:
Hai studiato:

  • Present Simple → routine
  • Present Continuous → adesso
  • Past Simple → passato
  • Going to / Will → futuro
  • Some / Any / Much / Many
  • Comparatives
  • Have you ever…?

👉 Ora li usiamo insieme.

docs.title: 📚 Vocabulary Review
docs.text:
Sai parlare di:

  • vita quotidiana
  • viaggi
  • cibo
  • città
  • esperienze
  • piani

👉 Ora devi combinare tutto.

docs.title: 📝 TEST 1 – Grammar
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ (work) every day
  2. I ___ (work) now
  3. Yesterday I ___ (go) to Rome
  4. I have ___ (eat) sushi
  5. I am going ___ (travel) tomorrow
  6. I will ___ (call) you
  7. I have ___ apples
  8. I don’t have ___ money
  9. This is ___ (big) than that
  10. This is the ___ (best) place

docs.title: 📝 TEST 2 – Trasformazioni
docs.text:
1. I work → (now) ______
  2. I go → (past) ______
  3. I eat → (present perfect) ______
  4. I travel tomorrow → ______
  5. I have apples → (negative) ______

docs.title: 🗣️ SPEAKING TEST (fondamentale)
docs.text:
👉 Parla per 2 minuti

Devi includere:

  • informazioni personali
  • routine
  • cosa stai facendo ora
  • cosa hai fatto ieri
  • esperienze
  • piani futuri

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: Hi, my name is Marco. I live in Italy and I work in an office. Every day I wake up at 7. Now I am studying English. Yesterday I went to the city and I ate pizza. I have been to Spain. Tomorrow I am going to work and I will study.

docs.title: ✍️ WRITING TEST
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (10–12 frasi):

Deve includere:

  • routine
  • passato
  • esperienza
  • futuro

docs.title: 🧠 THINK & SPEAK
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What do you do every day?
  • What are you doing now?
  • What did you do yesterday?
  • Have you ever travelled?
  • What are you going to do tomorrow?

docs.title: 🔊 PRONUNCIATION CHECK
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I am working now
  • I worked yesterday
  • I have been to Italy
  • I will call you

👉 fluido e naturale

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (check finale)
docs.text:
❌ I work now ✅ I am working now

❌ I have went ✅ I have gone

❌ I will to go ✅ I will go

❌ I don’t have some money ✅ I don’t have any money

docs.title: 🚀 FINAL TASK (livello reale)
docs.text:
Simula una conversazione:

👉 incontri qualcuno

Devi:

  • presentarti
  • fare 3 domande
  • parlare del passato
  • parlare del futuro

docs.title: 🎯 Autovalutazione
docs.text:
Se riesci a:

✅ parlare per 2 minuti ✅ capire domande semplici ✅ rispondere senza tradurre troppo ✅ scrivere un testo breve

👉 sei A2 ✔️

docs.title: 💬 Next step
docs.text:
👉 Sei pronto per B1

Nel prossimo livello imparerai:

  • conversazioni più lunghe
  • opinioni
  • condizioni (if...)
  • inglese più naturale

If you can talk about past, present, and future, you are no longer a beginner.

docs.title: 🟡 Livello B1 – Intermediate
docs.text: Qui diventi davvero autonomo.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo B1
docs.text:
• sostenere conversazioni reali
  • esprimere opinioni
  • scrivere email e messaggi
  • capire contenuti autentici semplici

docs.title: 📚 Cosa imparerai
docs.text:
• present perfect
  • future forms
  • conditional (base)
  • modali
  • strutture per opinioni

docs.title: 💡 Consiglio
docs.text:
Inizia a pensare direttamente in inglese.

👉 Continua con Unità 21

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare il present perfect in modo naturale
  • collegare passato e presente
  • parlare di esperienze, risultati e cambiamenti
  • evitare errori tipici italiani

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text:
👉 Subject + have/has + past participle

  • I have worked
  • She has studied
  • They have travelled

docs.title: 1. Esperienze
docs.text:
• I have been to Spain
  • I have eaten sushi

👉 (non dici quando)

docs.title: 2. Risultati (adesso)
docs.text:
• I have finished my work
  • I have lost my keys

👉 effetto nel presente

docs.title: 3. Cambiamenti
docs.text:
• I have learned English
  • She has improved a lot

docs.title: ⚖️ Present Perfect vs Past Simple
docs.text:
👉 differenza fondamentale:

Present Perfect Past Simple esperienza quando no tempo preciso tempo preciso

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have been to Rome (esperienza)
  • I went to Rome last year (quando)

docs.title: Past participles comuni
docs.text:
• go → gone
  • eat → eaten
  • see → seen
  • do → done
  • make → made
  • take → taken

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• already
  • just
  • yet

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have already finished
  • I have just eaten
  • I haven’t finished yet

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Have you finished your work? B: Yes, I have just finished.

A: Have you seen that film? B: No, I haven’t seen it yet.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I have finished
  • I have just eaten
  • I haven’t done it yet

👉 Ora parla:

  • I have ___
  • I haven’t ___ yet

👉 Fai domande:

  • Have you finished?
  • Have you seen it?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 usa:

  • already
  • just
  • yet

Esempio:

I have already finished my work. I have just eaten lunch. I haven’t watched that film yet.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • have → /həv/ (debole)
  • finished → /ˈfɪnɪʃt/

👉 parlato naturale:

  • I’ve finished
  • She’s done

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have went ✅ I have gone

❌ I have done yesterday ✅ I did it yesterday

❌ I am finished ✅ I have finished

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern mentale (CHIAVE B1)
docs.text:
👉 chiediti:

  • È importante quando? → Past Simple
  • È importante il risultato? → Present Perfect

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I have ___ (eat)
  2. She has ___ (go)
  3. I have ___ (see) that
  4. I haven’t ___ (finish) yet

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I finished → I have ______
  2. I ate → I have ______
  3. I saw → I have ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 3 cose che hai fatto oggi 👉 3 cose che non hai fatto

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 1–2 minuti:

👉 cosa hai fatto oggi

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Have you finished your work?
  • Have you eaten?
  • Have you seen that film?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • already
  • just
  • yet

👉 in 5 frasi

English becomes natural when you connect past and present.

docs.title: Unità 22 – Present Perfect vs Past Simple
🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare correttamente present perfect e past simple
  • capire quando usare uno o l’altro
  • evitare gli errori più comuni degli italiani
  • parlare in modo naturale

docs.title: 🧠 Differenza chiave (IMPORTANTISSIMA)
docs.text:
👉 chiediti sempre:

  • WHEN? (quando?) → Past Simple
  • RESULT / EXPERIENCE? → Present Perfect

docs.title: ⚖️ Confronto diretto
docs.text: Present Perfect Past Simple esperienza momento preciso senza tempo con tempo risultato evento finito

docs.title: Esperienza
docs.text: • I have been to Rome 👉 (non dici quando)

docs.title: Tempo preciso
docs.text: • I went to Rome last year 👉 (quando)

docs.title: Risultato
docs.text: • I have finished my work 👉 (importante adesso)

docs.title: Evento finito
docs.text: • I finished my work yesterday

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
• I have eaten → ho mangiato (non importa quando)
  • I ate at 8 → ho mangiato alle 8
  • I have seen that film → esperienza
  • I saw that film yesterday → quando

docs.title: Present Perfect
docs.text:
• already
  • just
  • yet
  • ever
  • never

docs.title: Past Simple
docs.text:
• yesterday
  • last week
  • in 2020
  • two days ago

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: Have you finished your work? B: Yes, I have finished.

A: When did you finish? B: I finished two hours ago.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I have finished
  • I finished yesterday

👉 Ora prova:

  • I have ___
  • I ___ yesterday

👉 Fai domande:

  • Have you finished?
  • When did you finish?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 3 present perfect 👉 3 past simple

Esempio:

I have eaten sushi. I have finished my work. I have seen that film.

I ate sushi yesterday. I finished my work at 6. I saw that film last week.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • I’ve finished
  • I finished

👉 differenza sottile ma importante

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have done yesterday ✅ I did it yesterday

❌ I went many times ✅ I have gone many times

❌ I have been in Rome in 2020 ✅ I went to Rome in 2020

docs.title: 🧠 Regola d’oro
docs.text:
👉 MAI usare Present Perfect con:

  • yesterday
  • last week
  • in 2020

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Scegli la forma corretta:

  1. I (have eaten / ate) yesterday
  2. I (have seen / saw) that film
  3. I (have finished / finished) two hours ago
  4. I (have been / went) to Spain

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I went to Rome → I have ______
  2. I ate sushi → I have ______
  3. I saw that film → I have ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 3 esperienze 👉 3 cose con tempo preciso

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 metà present perfect 👉 metà past simple

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Have you ever travelled?
  • When did you travel?
  • Have you finished your work?
  • When did you finish?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 5 coppie di frasi 👉 (present perfect + past simple)

The difference between these two tenses is the difference between basic and real English.

docs.title: Unità 23 – Future Forms (Will / Going to / Present Continuous)
🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare diversi modi per parlare del futuro
  • scegliere la forma corretta in base al contesto
  • parlare in modo più naturale e fluido
  • evitare errori tipici

docs.title: 1. Going to → piani
docs.text:
👉 decisione già presa

  • I am going to travel
  • I am going to study tonight

docs.title: 2. Will → decisione spontanea
docs.text:
👉 decido ora

  • I will help you
  • I will call you

docs.title: 3. Present Continuous → futuro organizzato
docs.text:
👉 appuntamenti / programmi

  • I am meeting a friend
  • I am travelling tomorrow

docs.title: ⚖️ Differenza chiave
docs.text: Forma Uso going to piano will decisione immediata present continuous programma organizzato

docs.title: Going to
docs.text: • I am going to study tonight 👉 già deciso

docs.title: Will
docs.text: • Ok, I will help you 👉 decisione ora

docs.title: Present Continuous
docs.text: • I am meeting my boss tomorrow 👉 appuntamento fissato

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What are you doing tomorrow? B: I am meeting a friend.

A: I’m tired. B: I will help you.

A: What are you going to do this weekend? B: I am going to travel.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I will call you
  • I am going to study
  • I am meeting a friend

👉 Ora prova:

  • I will ___
  • I am going to ___
  • I am ___ tomorrow

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 2 will 👉 2 going to 👉 2 present continuous

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Forma naturale:

  • I will → I’ll
  • I am going to → I’m gonna
  • I am meeting → I’m meeting

👉 parlato fluido = fondamentale B1

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I will to go ✅ I will go

❌ I going to travel ✅ I am going to travel

❌ I am go tomorrow ✅ I am going tomorrow

❌ I will meeting ✅ I will meet

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern mentale
docs.text:
👉 chiediti:

  • È pianificato? → going to
  • È deciso ora? → will
  • È organizzato? → present continuous

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
• I am going to travel → piano
  • I am travelling tomorrow → già organizzato
  • Ok, I will travel → decisione ora

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Scegli la forma corretta:

  1. I (will / am going to) study tonight (già deciso)
  2. Ok, I (will / am going to) help you
  3. I (am meeting / will meet) my friend tomorrow
  4. I (will go / am going) now

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I decided now → I ______ help
  2. I have a plan → I am ______ to travel
  3. I have a meeting → I am ______ a client

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa farai domani 👉 cosa hai già organizzato 👉 cosa decidi ora

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 i tuoi piani futuri

usa: - will - going to - present continuous

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What are you going to do tomorrow?
  • What will you do tonight?
  • What are you doing this weekend?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 3 piani
  • 2 decisioni spontanee
  • 2 appuntamenti

Real English uses different futures, not just “will”.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare di situazioni future possibili
  • usare frasi con if in modo corretto
  • fare previsioni e dare consigli
  • costruire frasi logiche

docs.title: Struttura base
docs.text:
👉 If + present simple, will + verb

  • If I study, I will pass
  • If it rains, I will stay home

docs.title: 🔁 Ordine delle frasi
docs.text:
👉 puoi invertire:

  • If I study, I will pass
  • I will pass if I study

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• If I work hard, I will succeed
  • If you eat too much, you will feel bad
  • If it rains, we will stay home

docs.title: Verbi utili
docs.text:
• study
  • work
  • eat
  • go
  • stay
  • help

docs.title: Espressioni utili
docs.text:
• if
  • when
  • maybe
  • probably

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: What will you do if it rains? B: If it rains, I will stay home.

A: What will you do if you have time? B: I will go to the gym.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • If I study, I will pass
  • If it rains, I will stay home

👉 Ora prova:

  • If I ___, I will ___

👉 Fai domande:

  • What will you do if ___?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 usa if

Esempio:

If I study, I will pass the exam. If it rains, I will stay home. If I have time, I will go out.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • if → /ɪf/
  • will → spesso debole (I’ll)

👉 parlato naturale:

  • If I study, I’ll pass

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ If I will study, I will pass ✅ If I study, I will pass

❌ If it will rain, I will stay home ✅ If it rains, I will stay home

❌ If I study, I pass ✅ If I study, I will pass

docs.title: 🧠 Regola fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 dopo if:

❌ NO futuro ✅ sempre present simple

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern mentale
docs.text:
👉 pensa così:

  • situazione possibile → risultato

👉 causa → effetto

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
• If I study → I will pass
  • If I don’t study → I will fail

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. If I ___ (study), I will pass
  2. If it ___ (rain), I will stay home
  3. If I ___ (have) time, I will go out
  4. If you ___ (eat) too much, you will feel bad

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I study → If I ______
  2. I go out → If I ______
  3. I eat → If I ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa farai se:

  • hai tempo
  • sei stanco
  • piove

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 crea 5 situazioni con if

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What will you do if it rains?
  • What will you do if you are tired?
  • What will you do if you have free time?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 5 frasi positive
  • 2 negative

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1 reale)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • probably
  • maybe

👉 esempio:

  • If I have time, I will probably go out

English becomes powerful when you can express consequences.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • esprimere opinioni in modo naturale
  • essere d’accordo o in disaccordo
  • sostenere una conversazione reale
  • usare frasi più fluide e naturali

docs.title: Esprimere opinioni
docs.text:
👉 strutture base:

  • I think...
  • I believe...
  • In my opinion...

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I think this film is good
  • I believe English is important
  • In my opinion, this is the best option

docs.title: Agreement (accordo)
docs.text:
• I agree
  • You’re right
  • I think so

docs.title: Disagreement (disaccordo)
docs.text:
• I don’t agree
  • I don’t think so
  • I think you’re wrong

docs.title: Opinioni più naturali
docs.text:
• I think it’s interesting
  • I don’t think it’s a good idea
  • It’s better to...

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: I think this restaurant is very good. B: Yes, I agree.

A: I think it’s expensive. B: I don’t agree. I think it’s ok.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I think it’s good
  • I don’t think it’s good
  • I agree
  • I don’t agree

👉 Ora prova:

  • I think ___
  • I don’t think ___

👉 Interazione:

  • Do you agree?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 esprimi opinioni

Esempio:

I think English is important. I think this city is beautiful. I don’t think this is a good idea.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • I think → /aɪ θɪŋk/ 👉 suono TH (molto importante)

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I think yes ✅ I think so

❌ I agree with you (sempre) 👉 meglio variare: ✅ You’re right

❌ I am agree ✅ I agree

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I think...
  • I don’t think...

👉 struttura base per parlare

docs.title: 🔁 Espansione (B1 reale)
docs.text:
👉 aggiungi:

  • because

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I think it’s good because it’s cheap
  • I don’t think it’s a good idea because it’s expensive

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ it’s good
  2. I don’t ___ it’s good
  3. I ___ with you
  4. I don’t ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. It is good → I think ______
  2. It is bad → I don’t think ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 esprimi opinioni su:

  • food
  • travel
  • work
  • English

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 dai 3 opinioni + spiegazione

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Do you think English is important?
  • Do you think travelling is useful?
  • Do you agree?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 3 opinioni
  • 2 disaccordi
  • usa because

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • I think...
  • I believe...
  • In my opinion...

👉 per variare

English becomes real when you can express your opinion.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare del tuo lavoro o dei tuoi studi
  • descrivere responsabilità e attività
  • sostenere una conversazione base professionale
  • rispondere a domande tipiche (anche da colloquio)

docs.title: 🧠 Grammar in context
docs.text:
👉 usiamo:

  • Present Simple → lavoro abituale
  • Present Perfect → esperienza
  • Future → piani

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I work in an office
  • I have worked in this company for 2 years
  • I am going to change job

docs.title: Work
docs.text:
• job
  • company
  • office
  • manager
  • colleague
  • salary
  • meeting

docs.title: Study
docs.text:
• study
  • course
  • exam
  • university
  • teacher
  • student

docs.title: Verbi utili
docs.text:
• work
  • study
  • learn
  • manage
  • help
  • organize

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue (job interview):

A: What do you do? B: I work in a company.

A: What are your responsibilities? B: I manage projects and help clients.

A: How long have you worked there? B: I have worked there for two years.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I work in an office
  • I study at university
  • I manage projects
  • I help people

👉 Ora parla:

  • I work as ___
  • I study ___
  • I help ___

👉 Rispondi:

  • What do you do?
  • Where do you work?

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6–8 frasi:

👉 parla del tuo lavoro o studio

Esempio:

I work in an office. I help clients. I have worked here for two years. I study English in my free time.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • work → /wɜːrk/
  • study → /ˈstʌdi/

👉 non leggere “study” come italiano

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I do a work ✅ I work

❌ I make a job ✅ I have a job

❌ I study to university ✅ I study at university

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I work as ___
  • I work in ___
  • I am responsible for ___

docs.title: 🔁 Espansione (B1 reale)
docs.text:
👉 usa:

  • I am responsible for...
  • I deal with...

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I am responsible for projects
  • I deal with clients

docs.title: ❓ Domande tipiche
docs.text:
• What do you do?
  • Where do you work?
