  • What do you study?
  • What are your responsibilities?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ in a company
  2. I ___ English
  3. I have ___ (work) here for 2 years
  4. I am responsible ___ projects

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I work → I have ______ (experience)
  2. I study → I am ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 il tuo lavoro 👉 cosa fai ogni giorno 👉 cosa vuoi fare in futuro

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula:

👉 un colloquio di lavoro

Rispondi:

  • What do you do?
  • What are your responsibilities?
  • What are your future plans?

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Do you work or study?
  • What do you do every day?
  • What do you want to do in the future?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 descrivi:

  • lavoro
  • esperienza
  • piani futuri

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1+)
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • because
  • so

👉 esempio:

  • I like my job because it is interesting

English is powerful when you can talk about your work and your future.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • dare consigli
  • esprimere obblighi e necessità
  • parlare di regole
  • usare must, should e have to correttamente

docs.title: Should → consigli
docs.text:
👉 per dire cosa è una buona idea

  • You should study more
  • You should sleep early

docs.title: Must → obbligo forte / regole
docs.text:
👉 forte, spesso personale

  • You must stop
  • You must study

docs.title: Have to → obbligo reale
docs.text:
👉 regole esterne / necessità

  • I have to work
  • I have to wake up early

docs.title: ⚖️ Differenza chiave
docs.text: Modal Uso should consiglio must obbligo forte have to obbligo reale

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• You should study English every day
  • You must wear a seatbelt
  • I have to work tomorrow

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: I’m tired. B: You should sleep more.

A: I have an exam. B: You must study.

A: I have a job. B: I have to work every day.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • You should study
  • You must stop
  • I have to work

👉 Ora prova:

  • You should ___
  • You must ___
  • I have to ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 2 consigli 👉 2 obblighi 👉 2 necessità

Esempio:

You should study more. You should eat healthy food. I must finish this work. You must stop here. I have to wake up early. I have to go to work.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • should → /ʃʊd/
  • must → /mʌst/

👉 suoni brevi e veloci

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I must to go ✅ I must go

❌ I have to go ✅ (corretto)

❌ You should to study ✅ You should study

👉 dopo modali → verbo base (no “to”)

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• Subject + modal + verb

👉 sempre senza “to”

docs.title: 🔁 Negativo
docs.text:
• You shouldn’t smoke
  • You mustn’t do that
  • I don’t have to work today

docs.title: 📚 Differenza negativa (importante)
docs.text:
• mustn’t → proibizione
  • don’t have to → non necessario

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• You mustn’t smoke → vietato
  • You don’t have to work → non serve

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. You ___ study more
  2. I ___ work tomorrow
  3. You ___ stop here
  4. You ___ smoke (consiglio negativo)

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I go → I must ______
  2. I work → I have to ______
  3. You study → You should ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 consigli per:

  • studiare
  • vivere meglio
  • lavorare

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 dai 5 consigli a una persona

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What should you do to learn English?
  • What do you have to do every day?
  • What must you do at work?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 3 consigli
  • 2 obblighi
  • 2 proibizioni

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • You should try...
  • You shouldn’t...

English becomes powerful when you can give advice and rules.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • scrivere email semplici
  • distinguere tra formale e informale
  • scrivere messaggi naturali
  • comunicare in contesti reali (lavoro, amici)

docs.title: 🧠 Grammar in context
docs.text:
👉 qui usiamo:

  • Present Simple
  • Present Perfect
  • Future

👉 ma soprattutto: - struttura e tono

docs.title: Informale (amici)
docs.text:
• Hi!
  • How are you?
  • See you soon

docs.title: Formale (lavoro)
docs.text:
• Dear Mr Smith,
  • I hope you are well
  • Best regards

docs.title: 1. Apertura
docs.text:
• Hi John, (informale)
  • Dear Mr Smith, (formale)

docs.title: 2. Corpo
docs.text:
• I am writing to...
  • I would like to...

docs.title: 3. Chiusura
docs.text:
• See you soon (informale)
  • Best regards (formale)

docs.title: Informale
docs.text:
• How are you?
  • I hope you are well
  • Let’s meet
  • See you soon

docs.title: Formale
docs.text:
• I am writing to inform you...
  • I would like to ask...
  • Thank you for your time

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Email (informale):

Hi John, How are you? I am writing to invite you to dinner. Let me know. See you soon!

Email (formale):

Dear Mr Smith, I hope you are well. I am writing to ask about the meeting. Thank you for your time. Best regards, Marco

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 anche se è scritto → leggilo ad alta voce:

  • I am writing to...
  • I would like to...

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text: Scrivi:

docs.title: 1 email informale
docs.text: 👉 a un amico

docs.title: 1 email formale
docs.text: 👉 a un collega / azienda

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • writing → /ˈraɪtɪŋ/
  • regards → /rɪˈɡɑːrdz/

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ Dear friend ✅ Hi / Hello

❌ I wait your answer ✅ I look forward to your reply

❌ I write you ✅ I am writing to you

docs.title: Email formale:
docs.text:
• I am writing to...
  • I would like to...

docs.title: Email informale:
docs.text:
• Hi!
  • How are you?
  • Let’s...

docs.title: 🔁 Differenza chiave
docs.text: Informale Formale Hi Dear See you Best regards Let’s meet I would like to meet

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. Hi John, how ___ you?
  2. I am writing ___ ask...
  3. Dear Mr Smith, I hope you ___ well
  4. Best ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. Hi → ______ (formale)
  2. See you → ______ (formale)
  3. I want → I would like ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 cosa scriveresti:

  • a un amico
  • a un’azienda

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 1 email completa (5–6 frasi)

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • How do you start an email?
  • What do you write in a formal email?
  • What is different from informal messages?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 2 email:

  • 1 informale
  • 1 formale

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • I look forward to your reply
  • Please let me know

English is not only spoken. It is written every day.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • capire cosa sono i phrasal verbs
  • usare i più comuni nella vita quotidiana
  • parlare in modo più naturale
  • riconoscere significati non letterali

docs.title: 🧠 Cosa sono i phrasal verbs?
docs.text:
👉 verbo + particella (preposizione / avverbio)

  • get up
  • go on
  • look for
  • find out

👉 spesso il significato NON è letterale

  • look for ≠ guardare per 👉 significa “cercare”

docs.title: Daily life
docs.text:
• get up → alzarsi
  • wake up → svegliarsi
  • go out → uscire
  • come back → tornare

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
• look for → cercare
  • find out → scoprire
  • turn on → accendere
  • turn off → spegnere

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I get up at 7
  • I go out with friends
  • I look for my keys
  • I turn on the TV

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I wake up at 7 and I get up. I turn on the coffee machine. I go out at 8. In the evening, I come back home.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I get up
  • I wake up
  • I go out
  • I come back

👉 Ora prova:

  • I ___ at 7
  • I ___ with friends
  • I ___ my keys

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 usa phrasal verbs

Esempio:

I get up at 7. I go out in the evening. I turn on the TV. I look for my phone.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • get up → /ɡet ʌp/
  • wake up → /weɪk ʌp/

👉 fluido, senza pausa forte

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I search my keys ✅ I look for my keys

❌ I exit with friends ✅ I go out with friends

❌ I return home 👉 corretto ma meno naturale ✅ I come back home

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 phrasal verbs = unità unica

NON tradurre parola per parola

docs.title: 🔁 Separabili / non separabili (base)
docs.text:
👉 semplice:

  • turn on the TV
  • turn the TV on

👉 entrambi corretti

docs.title: ❗ Attenzione
docs.text: • look for → NON separabile ❌ look my keys for ✅ look for my keys

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ up at 7
  2. I ___ out in the evening
  3. I ___ for my phone
  4. I ___ on the TV

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I wake → I ______ up
  2. I search → I ______ for
  3. I return → I ______ back

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 la tua giornata usando phrasal verbs

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 2 minuti:

👉 descrivi la tua routine

usa almeno: - 5 phrasal verbs

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What time do you get up?
  • Do you go out often?
  • What do you do when you come back home?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 5 frasi con phrasal verbs
  • 2 domande

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B1+)
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • sit down
  • stand up
  • take off
  • put on

Phrasal verbs are the key to natural English.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare tutti i tempi principali con sicurezza
  • sostenere una conversazione reale
  • esprimere opinioni, esperienze e piani
  • parlare in modo fluido (non perfetto, ma naturale)

docs.title: 🧠 Grammar Review
docs.text:
Hai studiato:

  • Present Simple → routine
  • Present Continuous → adesso / futuro
  • Past Simple → eventi passati
  • Present Perfect → esperienze / risultati
  • Future (will / going to / present continuous)
  • First Conditional (if...)
  • Modals (should, must, have to)
  • Phrasal verbs (base)

👉 Ora li usi insieme.

docs.title: 📚 Communication Skills
docs.text:
Sai:

  • parlare di te
  • raccontare esperienze
  • esprimere opinioni
  • dare consigli
  • parlare del futuro
  • scrivere email

👉 Ora devi integrarli.

docs.title: 📝 TEST 1 – Grammar
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ (work) every day
  2. I ___ (work) now
  3. Yesterday I ___ (go) out
  4. I have ___ (eat) sushi
  5. I ___ (go) to Rome last year
  6. I am going ___ (travel) tomorrow
  7. I will ___ (call) you
  8. If I ___ (study), I will pass
  9. You should ___ (study)
  10. I ___ up at 7 (phrasal verb)

docs.title: 📝 TEST 2 – Mixed Practice
docs.text:
Scegli la forma corretta:

  1. I (have eaten / ate) yesterday
  2. I (am working / work) now
  3. If it (rains / will rain), I will stay home
  4. I (have been / went) to Spain
  5. I (will help / am going to help) you (decisione ora)

docs.title: 🗣️ SPEAKING TEST (CRUCIALE)
docs.text:
👉 Parla per 3 minuti

Devi includere:

  • informazioni personali
  • routine
  • cosa stai facendo ora
  • cosa hai fatto recentemente
  • esperienze
  • opinioni
  • piani futuri

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: Hi, my name is Marco. I live in Italy and I work in an office. Every day I wake up at 7 and I go to work. Now I am studying English. Yesterday I went out with friends. I have been to Spain and I really liked it. I think travelling is very important because you learn a lot. Tomorrow I am going to work and I will study in the evening.

docs.title: ✍️ WRITING TEST
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (12–15 frasi):

Deve includere:

  • routine
  • passato
  • esperienza
  • opinione
  • futuro

docs.title: 🧠 THINK & SPEAK
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What do you do every day?
  • What did you do yesterday?
  • Have you ever travelled?
  • What do you think about learning English?
  • What will you do tomorrow?

docs.title: 🔊 PRONUNCIATION CHECK
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I’ve finished my work
  • I went out yesterday
  • I’m going to travel
  • If I study, I’ll pass

👉 fluido, senza pause

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (check finale)
docs.text:
❌ I have done yesterday ✅ I did it yesterday

❌ If I will study ✅ If I study

❌ I will to go ✅ I will go

❌ I search my keys ✅ I look for my keys

docs.title: 🚀 FINAL TASK (livello reale)
docs.text:
Simula una conversazione:

👉 incontri qualcuno

Devi:

  • presentarti
  • fare 3 domande
  • esprimere un’opinione
  • raccontare un’esperienza
  • parlare del futuro

docs.title: 🎯 Autovalutazione
docs.text:
Se riesci a:

✅ parlare per 3 minuti ✅ capire conversazioni semplici ✅ esprimere opinioni ✅ scrivere un testo strutturato

👉 sei B1 ✔️

docs.title: 💬 Next step
docs.text:
👉 Sei pronto per B2

Nel prossimo livello imparerai:

  • parlare in modo più fluente
  • discutere idee complesse
  • usare inglese più naturale e avanzato

If you can communicate, you are learning. If you can continue, you are fluent.

docs.title: 🔴 Livello B2 – Upper-Intermediate
docs.text: A questo livello sviluppi fluidità e precisione.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo B2
docs.text:
• parlare con sicurezza
  • argomentare
  • capire contenuti complessi
  • scrivere testi strutturati

docs.title: 📚 Cosa imparerai
docs.text:
• conditional avanzati
  • forma passiva
  • linking words
  • phrasal verbs
  • strutture complesse

docs.title: 💡 Consiglio
docs.text:
Lavora molto su:

  • speaking
  • listening reale
  • esposizione alla lingua

👉 Continua con Unità 31

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • parlare più fluentemente
  • evitare pause e blocchi
  • mantenere una conversazione più lunga
  • sembrare più naturale

docs.title: 🧠 Problema principale (livello B1 → B2)
docs.text:
👉 lo studente:

  • sa cosa dire ✔️
  • ma parla lentamente ❌

👉 soluzione: strategie di fluency

docs.title: 🧩 Tecnica 1 – Fillers
docs.text:
👉 parole per guadagnare tempo

  • Well...
  • You know...
  • I mean...
  • Let me think...

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Well, I think it’s a good idea
  • Let me think... maybe tomorrow

docs.title: 🧩 Tecnica 2 – Chunking
docs.text:
👉 parla in blocchi, non parola per parola

❌ I / think / it / is / good ✅ I think it’s a good idea

docs.title: 🧩 Tecnica 3 – Expanding ideas
docs.text:
👉 aggiungi dettagli

  • I like travelling ➡️ I like travelling because I can learn new things

docs.title: 🧩 Tecnica 4 – Rephrasing
docs.text:
👉 se non sai una parola, spiega

  • It’s a place where you eat 👉 (restaurant)

docs.title: 🧩 Tecnica 5 – Linking ideas
docs.text:
👉 usa connettori

  • and
  • but
  • because
  • so

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I like this city because it’s beautiful and there are many things to do.

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

Well, I think travelling is very important because you learn a lot. I mean, you see new cultures and meet new people.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • Well, I think...
  • I mean...
  • Let me think...

👉 Ora prova:

  • Well, I think ___
  • I mean ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing (fluency training)
docs.text:
Scrivi una frase base e migliorala:

👉 esempio:

  • I like English ➡️ I like English because it is useful and interesting

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 importante:

  • ritmo naturale
  • non parlare parola per parola

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ pause lunghe ❌ tradurre mentalmente

👉 soluzione: - usare fillers - parlare continuo

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 NON puntare a:

  • perfezione

👉 punta a:

  • continuità

docs.title: 🔁 Strategia reale
docs.text:
👉 meglio:

  • parlare con errori ✔️ che
  • non parlare ❌

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. Well, I ___
  2. I mean ___
  3. I think ___ because ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Espandi:

  1. I like food → ______
  2. I work → ______
  3. I study English → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 usa:

  • fillers
  • because
  • and

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 argomento libero

OBBLIGO: - usa almeno 3 fillers - usa because

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Why do you learn English?
  • What do you like?
  • What do you do in your free time?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 senza fermarti per 2 minuti

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2 reale)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • Actually...
  • Basically...

Fluency is not about perfection. It is about continuation.

docs.title: Unità 32 – Complex Sentences (and, but, because, although)
🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • costruire frasi più lunghe e naturali
  • collegare idee in modo fluido
  • esprimere contrasto e causa
  • parlare come un vero livello intermedio-alto

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è importante?
docs.text:
👉 livello base:

  • I like it
  • It is good

👉 livello B2:

  • I like it because it is interesting
  • It is good, but it is expensive

docs.title: AND → aggiungere
docs.text:
• I work and I study
  • I like coffee and tea

docs.title: BUT → contrasto
docs.text:
• I like it, but it is expensive
  • I want to go, but I am tired

docs.title: BECAUSE → causa
docs.text:
• I study because I want to improve
  • I am tired because I worked a lot

docs.title: ALTHOUGH → contrasto forte
docs.text:
• Although I am tired, I will go
  • Although it is expensive, I like it

docs.title: ⚖️ Differenza BUT vs ALTHOUGH
docs.text:
• but → più semplice
  • although → più formale / avanzato

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I like it, but it is expensive
  • Although it is expensive, I like it

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• I work during the day and I study in the evening
  • I like this city, but it is very busy
  • I study English because I need it for work
  • Although it is difficult, I enjoy learning English

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I work a lot, but I like my job because it is interesting. Although it is sometimes stressful, I learn many things.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I like it because...
  • I like it, but...
  • Although...

👉 Ora prova:

  • I ___ because ___
  • I ___ but ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 usa:

  • and
  • but
  • because
  • although

Esempio:

I work and I study. I like this job, but it is difficult. I study because I want to improve. Although it is hard, I continue.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • because → /bɪˈkɒz/
  • although → /ɔːlˈðəʊ/

👉 collega le parole (linking)

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ Although but ✅ usa solo uno

❌ I like it because is good ✅ I like it because it is good

❌ I study because for work ✅ I study because I need it for work

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 struttura:

  • idea + connector + idea

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 combina più idee:

  • I like this city because it is beautiful, but it is expensive

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I study ___ I want to improve
  2. I like it ___ it is expensive
  3. I work ___ I study
  4. ___ it is difficult, I continue

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Unisci:

  1. I work. I study → ______
  2. I like it. It is expensive → ______
  3. I study. I want to improve → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 usa almeno:

  • 2 because
  • 2 but
  • 1 although

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 descrivi:

  • lavoro
  • vita
  • obiettivi

usa connettori

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Why do you study English?
  • Do you like your job? Why?
  • What do you like and dislike?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 5 frasi lunghe (min 2 connettori)

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • so
  • however

Real English is not short. It is connected.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare combinazioni naturali di parole (collocations)
  • evitare traduzioni dall’italiano
  • parlare in modo più fluido e naturale
  • migliorare il tuo vocabolario reale

docs.title: 🧠 Cosa sono le collocations?
docs.text:
👉 parole che “stanno bene insieme”

  • make a decision
  • take a break
  • do homework

👉 NON puoi scegliere a caso

docs.title: ⚠️ Problema tipico
docs.text: ❌ traduzione dall’italiano ❌ make a photo ✅ take a photo

docs.title: Make
docs.text:
• make a decision
  • make a mistake
  • make money
  • make a plan

docs.title: Take
docs.text:
• take a break
  • take a photo
  • take a risk
  • take time

docs.title: Do
docs.text:
• do homework
  • do exercise
  • do business
  • do your best

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• I made a decision
  • I took a break
  • I did my homework

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I made an important decision yesterday. Then I took a break. In the evening, I did some work.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • make a decision
  • take a break
  • do homework

👉 Ora prova:

  • I made ___
  • I took ___
  • I did ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 usa collocations

Esempio:

I made a mistake. I took a break. I did my homework.

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 importante:

  • parole unite (chunking)

  • make-a-decision

  • take-a-break

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ make a photo ✅ take a photo

❌ do a mistake ✅ make a mistake

❌ make homework ✅ do homework

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text: 👉 NON imparare parole singole 👉 impara blocchi

docs.title: 🔁 Strategia efficace
docs.text:
👉 studia così:

❌ word = decision ✅ chunk = make a decision

docs.title: 📚 Collocations extra (B2)
docs.text:
• have a good time
  • get a job
  • give advice
  • pay attention

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. make a ___
  2. take a ___
  3. do ___ homework
  4. make a ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Correggi:

  1. make a photo → ______
  2. do a mistake → ______
  3. make homework → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 usa:

  • make
  • take
  • do

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 descrivi la tua giornata

usa almeno: - 5 collocations

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What decisions do you make?
  • When do you take a break?
  • What do you do every day?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 10 collocations
  • 5 frasi complete

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Aggiungi:

  • make progress
  • take responsibility
  • do your best

Natural English is not about words. It is about combinations.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • capire meglio l’inglese parlato
  • non bloccarti se non capisci una parola
  • spiegare qualcosa con parole diverse
  • mantenere una conversazione anche con difficoltà

docs.title: 🧠 Listening Strategy (fondamentale)
docs.text:
👉 NON ascoltare parola per parola

👉 ascolta:

  • idee
  • contesto
  • parole chiave

docs.title: ❌ Metodo sbagliato
docs.text:
• capire tutto
  • tradurre

docs.title: ✅ Metodo corretto
docs.text:
• capire il senso generale
  • ignorare dettagli non importanti

docs.title: 📚 Esempio
docs.text:
I went to a restaurant yesterday and it was amazing.

👉 anche se non capisci tutto:

  • went → passato
  • restaurant → luogo
  • amazing → positivo

👉 hai capito ✔️

docs.title: 🧩 Paraphrasing (abilità chiave)
docs.text: 👉 dire la stessa cosa in modo diverso

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• It is expensive ➡️ It costs a lot
  • I am tired ➡️ I feel tired
  • I like it ➡️ It is interesting for me

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è fondamentale?
docs.text:
👉 quando:

  • non trovi una parola
  • non capisci bene

👉 puoi continuare comunque ✔️

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I went to a very nice restaurant. The food was great and the service was excellent.

👉 Parafrasi:

I went to a good place to eat. The food was very good and people were very kind.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • It is expensive → It costs a lot
  • I am tired → I feel tired

👉 Ora prova:

  • It is difficult → ______
  • I like it → ______

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 5 frasi + parafrasi

Esempio:

It is interesting → It is very nice

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 importante:

  • parlare fluido
  • non fermarsi

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ blocco quando non capisci ❌ traduzione mentale

👉 soluzione: - continua - usa parole semplici

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 NON serve:

  • parola perfetta

👉 serve:

  • comunicare

docs.title: 🔁 Strategie pratiche
docs.text:
👉 se non sai una parola:

  • spiegala
  • descrivila
  • usa sinonimi

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: ❌ I don’t know the word ✅ It is a thing you use to...

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. It is expensive → It ______
  2. I am tired → I ______
  3. It is good → It ______

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Parafrasa:

  1. It is difficult → ______
  2. I like this place → ______
  3. I am hungry → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 descrivi qualcosa usando parole semplici

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 descrivi:

  • un film
  • un posto
  • un’esperienza

OBBLIGO: - usa parafrasi

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Can you explain this in another way?
  • What do you mean?
  • Can you say it differently?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Spiega:

👉 3 cose senza usare la parola principale

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • It is like...
  • It is similar to...

Fluency is not knowing every word. It is knowing how to continue without them.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • esprimere accordo e disaccordo
  • reagire alle opinioni degli altri
  • sostenere un’idea
  • partecipare a discussioni reali

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è importante?
docs.text:
👉 livello B1:

  • I think it’s good

👉 livello B2:

  • I agree, but I think it could be better

👉 interazione vera

docs.title: 📚 Agreement (accordo)
docs.text:
• I agree
  • I totally agree
  • You’re right
  • That’s true

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I agree with you
  • You’re right, it’s a good idea

docs.title: 📚 Disagreement (disaccordo)
docs.text:
👉 soft (più naturale)

  • I’m not sure
  • I don’t think so
  • I see your point, but...

👉 strong

  • I don’t agree
  • I think you’re wrong

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I see your point, but I think it’s expensive
  • I don’t think that’s a good idea

docs.title: 📚 Giving reasons
docs.text:
👉 per argomentare

  • because
  • I think... because...
  • the reason is...

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I think it’s good because it’s useful
  • I don’t agree because it’s too expensive

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Dialogue:

A: I think working from home is better. B: I see your point, but I prefer working in an office.

A: Why? B: Because I can focus better.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I agree
  • I don’t agree
  • I see your point, but...

👉 Ora prova:

  • I think ___
  • I agree, but ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 6 frasi:

👉 3 accordi 👉 3 disaccordi

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
Attenzione:

  • agree → /əˈɡriː/
  • point → /pɔɪnt/

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I am agree ✅ I agree

❌ I don’t agree with this idea because is bad ✅ I don’t agree because it is bad

❌ I think yes ✅ I think so

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
• I agree, but...
  • I see your point, but...

👉 chiave per discussioni naturali

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 aggiungi:

  • actually
  • in my opinion

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Actually, I don’t agree
  • In my opinion, it’s better

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ with you
  2. I don’t ___
  3. I see your ___, but...
  4. I think ___ because ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. It is good → I agree, ______
  2. It is bad → I don’t think ______
  3. It is expensive → I see your point, ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 dai la tua opinione su:

  • lavoro
  • viaggi
  • tecnologia

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Simula:

👉 una discussione

  • esprimi un’opinione
  • reagisci
  • dai una ragione

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Do you agree with this idea?
  • Why or why not?
  • What do you think?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 esprimi: - 2 opinioni - 2 accordi - 2 disaccordi

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • I partly agree
  • It depends

Real English is not just speaking. It is interacting.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • scrivere paragrafi chiari e strutturati
  • organizzare le idee in modo logico
  • usare connettori avanzati
  • scrivere testi più naturali e professionali

docs.title: 🧠 Problema tipico
docs.text:
👉 livello basso:

  • frasi scollegate
  • idee confuse

👉 livello B2:

  • testo organizzato
  • idee collegate
  • struttura chiara

docs.title: 1. Topic sentence
docs.text:
👉 idea principale

  • I think travelling is very important

docs.title: 2. Supporting ideas
docs.text:
👉 spiegazioni

  • because you can learn new cultures
  • and meet new people

docs.title: 3. Conclusion
docs.text:
👉 chiusura

  • so it is a very useful experience

docs.title: 📚 Esempio completo
docs.text: I think travelling is very important because you can learn new cultures and meet new people. For this reason, it is a very useful experience.

docs.title: 🔗 Linking words (B2)
docs.text:
• first
  • then
  • also
  • however
  • for this reason
  • in conclusion

docs.title: 📚 Esempi
docs.text:
• First, I studied English. Then I worked abroad.
  • It is useful. However, it is difficult.
  • For this reason, I continue learning.

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I think learning English is important because it helps you find better opportunities. Also, you can travel more easily. For this reason, many people study English.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking → Writing bridge
docs.text:
👉 prima parla → poi scrivi

  • I think...
  • because...
  • so...

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un paragrafo (5–7 frasi):

👉 tema:

  • English
  • work
  • travel

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 utile per scrittura:

  • leggere ad alta voce → controlla se suona naturale

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text: ❌ frasi troppo corte ❌ ripetizione (very very very) ❌ nessuna connessione

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text: 👉 idea → spiegazione → conclusione

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 usa:

  • because
  • however
  • for this reason

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I think ___ because ___
  2. It is useful. However, ___
  3. For this reason, ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Unisci:

  1. I like English. It is useful → ______
  2. It is difficult. I continue → ______
  3. I study. I want to improve → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Write
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 un paragrafo su:

  • lavoro
  • studio
  • viaggi

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 8–10 frasi collegate

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Why is English important?
  • What do you think about working abroad?
  • What are the advantages of travelling?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 1 paragrafo completo con:

  • 1 idea principale
  • 2 spiegazioni
  • 1 conclusione

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • in addition
  • on the other hand

Writing is not about sentences. It is about structure.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • unire frasi in modo naturale
  • descrivere persone, cose e luoghi meglio
  • usare who, which, that
  • parlare in modo più fluido e sofisticato

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è importante?
docs.text:
👉 livello base:

  • I have a friend. He lives in London

👉 livello B2:

  • I have a friend who lives in London

👉 più naturale ✔️

docs.title: WHO → persone
docs.text:
• I have a friend who works in an office
  • She is the person who helped me

docs.title: WHICH → cose
docs.text:
• This is a book which is very interesting
  • I have a phone which is very fast

docs.title: THAT → persone + cose (più naturale)
docs.text:
• I have a friend that works in an office
  • This is a book that I like

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• The man who works here is very friendly
  • The car which I bought is expensive
  • The job that I have is interesting

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

I have a friend who lives in Spain. He has a job that is very interesting. He works for a company which sells technology.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I know a person who...
  • I have something that...
  • This is a place which...

👉 Ora prova:

  • I have a friend who ___
  • I like things that ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 5 frasi:

👉 usa:

  • who
  • which
  • that

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 importante:

  • who → /huː/
  • which → /wɪtʃ/

👉 spesso “that” è debole

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ I have a friend he works ✅ I have a friend who works

❌ The thing what I like ✅ The thing that I like

❌ The person which works ✅ The person who works

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text: 👉 nome + who/which/that + informazione

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 usa per evitare ripetizioni:

❌ I have a car. The car is fast ✅ I have a car that is fast

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia
docs.text:
👉 invece di:

  • 2 frasi 👉 usa:
  • 1 frase più naturale

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I have a friend ___ works in London
  2. This is a book ___ I like
  3. The person ___ helped me is here
  4. This is a place ___ is very nice

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Unisci:

  1. I have a friend. He works → ______
  2. I have a phone. It is fast → ______
  3. I like a place. It is quiet → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 descrivi:

  • una persona
  • un oggetto
  • un luogo

usa relative clauses

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3 minuti:

👉 descrivi:

  • il tuo lavoro
  • la tua città

usa: - who - that - which

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Who is a person who inspires you?
  • What is something that you like?
  • What is a place which is important for you?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Crea:

  • 5 frasi con who
  • 5 con that
  • 3 con which

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • where
  • whose

Advanced English is about connecting ideas, not separating them.

docs.title: Unità 38 – Nuance & Tone (Formal vs Informal English)
🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • distinguere tra inglese formale e informale
  • scegliere il tono corretto in base al contesto
  • adattare il tuo linguaggio
  • sembrare più naturale e professionale

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è importante?
docs.text:
👉 stessa idea → modi diversi

  • informale: Hi, can you help me?
  • formale: Could you please help me?

👉 cambia il tono, non il significato

docs.title: 📚 Informal English
docs.text:
👉 usato con:

  • amici
  • colleghi informali
  • chat

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Hi!
  • Thanks
  • I want
  • Let’s meet

docs.title: 📚 Formal English
docs.text:
👉 usato per:

  • lavoro
  • email professionali
  • situazioni ufficiali

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• Dear Sir/Madam
  • Thank you very much
  • I would like
  • I look forward to...

docs.title: ⚖️ Confronto diretto
docs.text: Informale Formale Hi Dear Thanks Thank you I want I would like Can you...? Could you please...?

docs.title: 📚 Esempi reali
docs.text:
• Informale: I want information
  • Formale: I would like some information
  • Informale: Can you send it?
  • Formale: Could you please send it?

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Examples:

Informal:

Hi, can you help me with this?

Formal:

Dear Mr Smith, Could you please help me with this?

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I want → I would like
  • Can you → Could you please

👉 Ora prova:

  • I want ___ → I would like ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Trasforma:

👉 da informale → formale

Esempio:

  • I want info → I would like some information

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 nel formale:

  • ritmo più lento
  • più chiaro

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ uso troppo diretto ❌ mancanza di cortesia

👉 esempio:

❌ Send me this ✅ Could you please send me this?

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text:
👉 formale:

  • I would like...
  • Could you please...?

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 aggiungi:

  • I was wondering if...
  • Would it be possible to...?

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I was wondering if you could help me
  • Would it be possible to change the time?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I want → I would ___
  2. Can you → Could you ___
  3. Thanks → Thank ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. Send me this → ______
  2. I want help → ______
  3. Can you call me? → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 stessa frase in:

  • informale
  • formale

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 1 email informale 👉 1 email formale

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • When do you use formal English?
  • When do you use informal English?
  • Which do you use more?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Trasforma:

👉 5 frasi informali → formali

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • I appreciate your help
  • Please let me know

Real English is not just correct. It is appropriate.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • raccontare storie in modo chiaro e interessante
  • strutturare un discorso
  • argomentare le tue idee
  • parlare più a lungo senza perdere il filo

docs.title: 🧠 Perché è importante?
docs.text:
👉 livello B1:

  • Yesterday I went out. It was nice.

👉 livello B2:

  • Yesterday I went out with friends and we had a great time because the place was really nice.

👉 più ricco ✔️

docs.title: Struttura base
docs.text:
1. Introduzione
  2. Evento
  3. Dettagli
  4. Conclusione

docs.title: 📚 Esempio
docs.text: Yesterday I went out with my friends. We went to a restaurant in the city. The food was amazing and the atmosphere was great. In the end, we had a really good time.

docs.title: 🔗 Linking words (fondamentali)
docs.text:
• first
  • then
  • after that
  • finally

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: First, we met at 8. Then we went to a restaurant. After that, we walked in the city. Finally, we went home.

docs.title: 🧠 Adding details
docs.text:
👉 rende il discorso interessante

  • very
  • really
  • a lot
  • quite

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• It was very interesting
  • The food was really good

docs.title: 🧩 Giving arguments
docs.text: 👉 esprimere e difendere idee

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text:
• I think...
  • because...
  • for example...

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I think travelling is important because you can learn new cultures. For example, when I went to Spain, I learned a lot.

docs.title: 🎧 Listening / Reading
docs.text:
Text:

First, I went to the city. Then I met my friends. After that, we had dinner. Finally, we went home. I think it was a great experience because I relaxed and enjoyed my time.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • First...
  • Then...
  • After that...
  • Finally...

👉 Ora prova:

  • First I ___
  • Then I ___

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi:

👉 una storia (6–8 frasi)

docs.title: 🔊 Pronunciation
docs.text:
👉 importante:

  • ritmo continuo
  • collegare le frasi

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (italiani)
docs.text:
❌ frasi troppo brevi ❌ niente connessioni ❌ ripetizione

👉 soluzione: - usare linking words - aggiungere dettagli

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern fondamentale
docs.text: 👉 evento → dettaglio → risultato

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (B2 reale)
docs.text:
👉 combina:

  • storytelling + opinione

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I went to a new restaurant and it was great. I think it’s one of the best places in the city because the food was excellent.

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. First, I ___
  2. Then I ___
  3. I think ___ because ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
Espandi:

  1. I went out → ______
  2. It was good → ______
  3. I liked it → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 racconta:

  • un’esperienza
  • una giornata
  • un viaggio

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3–4 minuti:

👉 racconta una storia completa

usa: - first / then / after that / finally - because

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What did you do last weekend?
  • Tell me about a good experience
  • Why was it good?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 4 minuti senza fermarti

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (B2+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • actually
  • in fact

Fluency is not just speaking. It is telling stories.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • sostenere una conversazione fluida
  • esprimere opinioni complesse
  • raccontare esperienze e storie
  • usare inglese naturale in contesti reali

docs.title: 🧠 Cosa sai fare ora
docs.text:
Hai imparato a:

  • usare tutti i tempi verbali
  • collegare idee
  • esprimere opinioni
  • discutere
  • raccontare storie
  • scrivere testi strutturati

👉 ora li usi insieme

docs.title: 📝 TEST 1 – Grammar & Vocabulary
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I have ___ (finish) my work
  2. Yesterday I ___ (go) out
  3. If I ___ (have) time, I will go
  4. I think it’s good ___ it is useful
  5. I made a ___ (decision)
  6. I have a friend ___ lives in London
  7. I am going ___ (travel) tomorrow
  8. You should ___ (study) more
  9. I ___ up at 7 (phrasal verb)
  10. I would like ___ (ask) you something

docs.title: 📝 TEST 2 – Transformation
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I want help → I would like ______
  2. I have a friend. He works → I have a friend ______
  3. I went out. It was nice → ______
  4. It is expensive → It ______ (paraphrase)

docs.title: 🗣️ SPEAKING TEST (FONDAMENTALE)
docs.text:
👉 Parla per 5 minuti

Devi includere:

  • introduzione personale
  • routine
  • esperienza passata
  • opinione
  • discussione (pro / contro)
  • futuro

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: Hi, my name is Marco and I live in Italy. I work in an office and I study English in my free time. Recently, I have improved my English a lot. I think learning English is very important because it gives you more opportunities. However, it can be difficult at the beginning. In the future, I am going to travel more and use English every day.

docs.title: ✍️ WRITING TEST
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (15–20 frasi):

Deve includere:

  • introduzione
  • sviluppo
  • conclusione

👉 usa:

  • because
  • however
  • for this reason

docs.title: 🧠 THINK & SPEAK
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What do you think about learning English?
  • What are the advantages and disadvantages?
  • What are your future plans?
  • What is an important experience in your life?

docs.title: 🔊 PRONUNCIATION CHECK
docs.text:
Ripeti:

  • I’ve been working on this
  • I would like to ask you something
  • I think it’s important
  • If I have time, I’ll go

👉 fluido e naturale

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (check finale)
docs.text:
❌ I am agree ✅ I agree

❌ I have done yesterday ✅ I did it yesterday

❌ If I will go ✅ If I go

❌ I make a photo ✅ I take a photo

docs.title: 🚀 FINAL TASK (livello reale)
docs.text:
Simula:

👉 una conversazione completa

Devi:

  • raccontare una storia
  • esprimere un’opinione
  • reagire a un’idea
  • fare una domanda

docs.title: 🎯 Autovalutazione
docs.text:
Se riesci a:

✅ parlare per 5 minuti ✅ capire conversazioni normali ✅ esprimere idee complesse ✅ scrivere testi strutturati

👉 sei B2 ✔️

docs.title: 💬 Next step
docs.text:
👉 Ora puoi:

  • lavorare in inglese
  • viaggiare senza difficoltà
  • sostenere conversazioni reali

docs.title: 🚀 Beyond B2
docs.text:
Per andare oltre:

  • ascolta contenuti reali
  • parla ogni giorno
  • leggi articoli

You are no longer learning English. You are using it.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo del livello C1
docs.text:
Il livello C1 rappresenta il passaggio da:

👉 “parlare bene inglese” a 👉 usare l’inglese con precisione, controllo e naturalezza

Alla fine di questo livello sarai in grado di:

  • esprimere idee complesse con chiarezza
  • parlare con fluidità e sicurezza
  • usare strutture avanzate in modo naturale
  • comprendere e produrre inglese in contesti reali
  • adattare il linguaggio a diverse situazioni

docs.title: 🧠 Il vero significato di C1
docs.text:
👉 non è più:

  • grammatica base
  • frasi semplici

👉 ma:

  • scelta delle parole
  • connessione delle idee
  • controllo della lingua

docs.title: 🔁 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text: B2 C1 comunichi bene comunichi con precisione usi grammatica controlli la grammatica parli esprimi idee capisci interpreti

docs.title: 📚 Struttura del livello C1
docs.text:
Il livello C1 è composto da 10 unità:

Unità Contenuto 41 Advanced Tenses (Nuance & Control) 42 Conditionals (Advanced & Mixed) 43 Modal Verbs (Speculation & Deduction) 44 Passive Voice (Advanced Use) 45 Reported Speech (Advanced Use) 46 Advanced Connectors 47 Collocations 48 Phrasal Verbs (Advanced) 49 Nuance & Precision 50 Speaking & Writing Mastery

docs.title: 🧩 Cosa imparerai davvero
docs.text: Durante questo livello svilupperai:

docs.title: 🧠 Precisione
docs.text:
• scegliere parole migliori
  • evitare espressioni generiche
  • comunicare con accuratezza

docs.title: 🔗 Struttura
docs.text:
• collegare idee
  • costruire discorsi
  • scrivere testi complessi

docs.title: 🗣️ Fluency avanzata
docs.text:
• parlare senza blocchi
  • sviluppare argomenti
  • esprimere opinioni

docs.title: 🎯 Controllo linguistico
docs.text:
• usare tempi con sfumature
  • combinare strutture
  • adattare il linguaggio

docs.title: 🧠 Metodo per il livello C1
docs.text: Per ottenere risultati reali:

docs.title: 📊 Metodo consigliato
docs.text: Step Azione 1 studia la lezione 2 analizza gli esempi 3 fai esercizi 4 parla ad alta voce 5 ripeti e consolida

docs.title: ⏱ Frequenza consigliata
docs.text:
👉 ideale:

  • 20–30 minuti al giorno
  • focus su speaking

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori da evitare
docs.text: Errore Soluzione usare parole base usa sinonimi frasi semplici sviluppa idee parlare poco parla ogni giorno tradurre dall’italiano pensa in inglese

docs.title: 🗣️ Focus sullo speaking
docs.text:
A livello C1 devi:

  • parlare per 4–5 minuti
  • sviluppare idee
  • usare connectors
  • usare collocations

👉 lo speaking è la priorità

docs.title: ✍️ Focus sul writing
docs.text:
Devi saper:

  • scrivere testi strutturati
  • usare introduzione, sviluppo, conclusione
  • collegare idee

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo finale C1
docs.text:
Alla fine del livello C1 sarai in grado di:

  • sostenere conversazioni complesse
  • esprimere opinioni articolate
  • capire inglese naturale
  • scrivere testi avanzati

docs.title: 🧩 Come usare questo livello
docs.text:
👉 non avere fretta

👉 ogni unità:

  • leggila
  • usala
  • parlala

docs.title: 🔁 Progressione ideale
docs.text: Giorno Attività Giorno 1 nuova unità Giorno 2 ripasso Giorno 3 speaking Giorno 4 writing

docs.title: 🚀 Dopo il C1
docs.text:
Dopo questo livello entrerai nel:

👉 C2 – Mastery

Dove lavorerai su:

  • naturalezza totale
  • espressioni idiomatiche
  • stile
  • pensiero avanzato

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text: 👉 A1–B2 = impari l’inglese 👉 C1 = controlli l’inglese

docs.title: 💡 Regola d’oro
docs.text:
👉 non cercare solo correttezza

👉 cerca:

  • precisione
  • naturalezza
  • fluidità

At C1 level, English is no longer a subject. It becomes a tool.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare i tempi verbali con precisione avanzata
  • capire le differenze sottili tra tempi simili
  • scegliere il tempo giusto in base al significato
  • parlare e scrivere con maggiore controllo

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • uso corretto dei tempi

👉 C1:

  • uso preciso dei tempi

👉 non è più:

✔️ “è giusto o sbagliato”

👉 ma:

✔️ “qual è più naturale?” ✔️ “qual è più preciso?”

docs.title: Base (già conosciuta)
docs.text:
• I went → tempo preciso
  • I have gone → esperienza / risultato

docs.title: Livello C1 (sfumatura)
docs.text:
👉 confronto:

  • I lived in London for 2 years 👉 esperienza finita

  • I have lived in London for 2 years 👉 esperienza collegata al presente

docs.title: Differenza reale
docs.text: Frase Significato I lived there non vivo più lì I have lived there potrebbe essere ancora vero

docs.title: 🧠 Present Perfect Continuous vs Simple
docs.text:
👉 confronto avanzato:

  • I have worked all day
  • I have been working all day

docs.title: Differenza
docs.text: Forma Focus Present Perfect risultato Present Perfect Continuous durata / processo

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I have written 5 emails 👉 risultato

  • I have been writing emails all morning 👉 attività continua

docs.title: 🔁 Past Continuous vs Past Simple
docs.text: 👉 non solo “azione in corso”

docs.title: Esempio avanzato
docs.text:
• I was working when he called 👉 contesto

  • I worked when he called 👉 sequenza (meno naturale)

docs.title: Uso C1
docs.text: 👉 Past Continuous = background

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I was walking through the city when I saw something strange.

docs.title: 🔮 Future Nuance (C1)
docs.text:
👉 confronto:

  • I will do it
  • I’m going to do it
  • I’m doing it

docs.title: Differenze reali
docs.text: Forma Uso will decisione / promessa going to intenzione present continuous piano organizzato

docs.title: Livello C1
docs.text:
👉 scegliere in base al contesto:

  • I’ll call you (decisione ora)
  • I’m going to call you (intenzione)
  • I’m calling you at 8 (programma)

docs.title: 🧠 Time Perspective (C1 Skill)
docs.text:
👉 pensa sempre:

  • quando succede?
  • è finito?
  • è ancora rilevante?

docs.title: 🧩 Esempio avanzato
docs.text:
I have been working here for 3 years, and I really enjoy it. Before that, I worked in a different company.

👉 perfetto mix tempi

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: I have been studying English for several years, and I have recently started to feel more confident. Before that, I struggled a lot and I didn’t understand native speakers.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa combinazioni:

  • I have been...
  • I have...
  • I used to...

👉 esempio:

I have been learning English for a long time, but I have improved a lot recently.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (8–12 frasi):

👉 includi:

  • presente
  • passato
  • esperienza
  • cambiamento

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (livello avanzato)
docs.text: Errore Corretto I am living here since 2 years I have been living here for 2 years I have worked yesterday I worked yesterday I work here since 2020 I have worked here since 2020

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 combinare tempi:

  • I have been working here and I really enjoy it
  • I worked there before I moved here

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text: 👉 usa più tempi nella stessa frase

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I have been working on this project for weeks, and I finally finished it yesterday.

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. I ___ (live) here since 2020
  2. I ___ (work) yesterday
  3. I have been ___ (study) all day
  4. Before that, I ___ (work) in another company

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. I live here (since 2020) → ______
  2. I study all day → ______
  3. I finished → aggiungi contesto

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 il tuo percorso:

  • prima
  • adesso
  • cambiamento

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3–4 minuti:

👉 usa almeno 3 tempi diversi

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • How has your English improved?
  • What did you do before?
  • What are you doing now?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Crea un discorso con:

  • present perfect
  • past simple
  • present continuous

👉 senza fermarti

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • I have recently...
  • I have been...
  • Before that...

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 A1–B2 = imparare i tempi 👉 C1 = controllare i tempi

At an advanced level, grammar is not about rules. It is about choices.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare tutti i tipi di conditional in modo naturale
  • combinare tempi diversi (mixed conditionals)
  • esprimere ipotesi complesse
  • parlare di possibilità, rimpianti e conseguenze

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • conosci le strutture

👉 C1:

  • le usi per pensare

👉 i conditionals non sono grammatica 👉 sono logica + realtà

docs.title: 0 Conditional (verità generale)
docs.text:
👉 If + present → present

  • If you heat water, it boils

docs.title: 1st Conditional (possibilità reale)
docs.text:
👉 If + present → will

  • If I study, I will pass

docs.title: 2nd Conditional (irreale presente)
docs.text:
👉 If + past → would

  • If I had time, I would travel

docs.title: 3rd Conditional (irreale passato)
docs.text:
👉 If + past perfect → would have

  • If I had studied, I would have passed

docs.title: 🔥 Il livello C1: Mixed Conditionals
docs.text:
👉 qui succede il vero salto

👉 mescoli:

  • passato
  • presente
  • risultato

docs.title: 🧩 Tipo 1 – Past → Present
docs.text: 👉 azione passata → effetto presente

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text: If + past perfect → would + base

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• If I had studied more, I would have a better job now
  • If I had moved abroad, I would speak better English

👉 significato:

❌ non ho studiato 👉 adesso ho conseguenze

docs.title: 🧩 Tipo 2 – Present → Past
docs.text: 👉 situazione attuale → effetto passato

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text: If + past → would have + past participle

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• If I were more organized, I would have finished earlier
  • If I knew the answer, I would have told you

👉 significato:

❌ non sono organizzato 👉 nel passato non ho finito

docs.title: 🧠 Differenze chiave
docs.text: Tipo Focus 2nd presente irreale 3rd passato irreale Mixed connessione tra tempi

docs.title: 🔁 Alternative avanzate
docs.text: 👉 a livello C1 non usi solo “if”

docs.title: Instead of “if”
docs.text:
• unless
  • as long as
  • provided that
  • even if

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I won’t go unless you come
  • I’ll help you as long as you try

docs.title: 🧠 Conditionals senza “if”
docs.text: 👉 struttura avanzata

docs.title: Inversion
docs.text:
• Had I known, I would have helped
  • Were I you, I would do it
  • Should you need help, call me

👉 molto formale / C2-like

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: If I had taken that opportunity, I would be living abroad now. However, I chose a different path, and although it wasn’t easy, it helped me grow.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa strutture:

  • If I had... I would...
  • If I were... I would...

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: If I had studied more, I would have better opportunities now.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (10–15 frasi):

👉 includi:

  • almeno 2 conditionals
  • almeno 1 mixed

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto If I would study If I studied If I will go If I go If I studied, I will pass If I study, I will pass

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 combinare idee:

  • If I had known, I would have done it differently
  • If I were you, I wouldn’t do that

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text:
👉 usa conditionals per:

  • opinioni
  • consigli
  • riflessioni

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• If I were you, I would reconsider
  • If I had known, I would have acted differently

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. If I ___ (study), I will pass
  2. If I ___ (have) time, I would travel
  3. If I had ___ (know), I would have helped
  4. If I had studied more, I ___ (have) a better job now

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. I didn’t study → ______
  2. I don’t have time → ______
  3. I didn’t go → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 3 situazioni:

  • passato (rimpianto)
  • presente (irreale)
  • mix

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 4 minuti:

👉 usa:

  • 2nd conditional
  • 3rd conditional
  • 1 mixed

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What would you do if you had more time?
  • What would you have done differently?
  • If you could change one thing, what would it be?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Crea:

👉 5 frasi mixed conditional

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • Had I known...
  • Were I you...

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 A2–B1: impari i conditionals 👉 B2: li usi 👉 C1: li combini

Conditionals are not grammar. They are how you think about reality.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • fare deduzioni logiche
  • esprimere certezza e probabilità
  • parlare di situazioni presenti e passate in modo sofisticato
  • usare i modal verbs come un madrelingua

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • can, should, must = regole

👉 C1:

  • modal verbs = interpretazione della realtà

👉 non descrivi più cosa succede 👉 ma cosa pensi che succeda

docs.title: 🔴 Must (certezza alta)
docs.text:
👉 sono sicuro

  • He must be tired
  • She must be at work

docs.title: 🟡 Might / May / Could (possibilità)
docs.text:
👉 non sono sicuro

  • He might be at home
  • She could be working

docs.title: ⚫ Can’t (impossibilità)
docs.text:
👉 sono sicuro che NON

  • He can’t be at home
  • This can’t be true

docs.title: 📊 Confronto
docs.text: Modal Significato must 90% sicuro might / may / could possibile can’t impossibile

docs.title: 🧠 Deduction (passato)
docs.text: 👉 qui diventa C1 vero

docs.title: 🔴 Must have + past participle
docs.text:
👉 quasi certo

  • He must have left early
  • She must have forgotten

docs.title: 🟡 Might / May / Could have
docs.text:
👉 possibile

  • He might have missed the train
  • She could have gone home

docs.title: ⚫ Can’t have
docs.text:
👉 impossibile

  • He can’t have done that
  • She can’t have seen it

docs.title: 🧩 Esempio reale
docs.text: The lights are off. He must have gone out.

docs.title: 🧠 Differenza chiave
docs.text: Tempo Uso must be presente must have been passato

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
• He must be tired 👉 adesso

  • He must have been tired 👉 nel passato

docs.title: 🧠 Speculation vs Reality
docs.text: 👉 non descrivi fatti 👉 descrivi interpretazioni

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text:
• She is late → fatto
  • She must be stuck in traffic → interpretazione

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: He didn’t answer the phone. He must have been busy, or he might have been in a meeting.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa:

  • must
  • might
  • can’t

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: He’s not here. He must be working, but he might be at home.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 10 frasi:

👉 includi:

  • 3 deduzioni presenti
  • 3 deduzioni passate
  • 4 miste

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto He must to be tired He must be tired He must has gone He must have gone He can’t to go He can’t go

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 struttura chiave:

  • modal + base
  • modal + have + past participle

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text: 👉 combina deduzioni:

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: He must have been tired because he didn’t answer, but he might have been working.

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia mentale
docs.text:
👉 chiediti:

  • cosa vedo?
  • cosa penso?
  • quanto sono sicuro?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. He ___ be tired (certezza)
  2. She ___ be at home (possibile)
  3. He ___ have forgotten (certezza passata)
  4. They ___ have gone (possibile)

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. He is tired → (deduzione) ______
  2. She left early → (deduzione) ______
  3. He didn’t answer → (deduzione) ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 crea ipotesi su:

  • una persona
  • una situazione
  • un evento passato

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 4 minuti:

👉 usa almeno:

  • 3 must
  • 2 might
  • 2 can’t

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Why didn’t he answer?
  • Where do you think she is?
  • What happened yesterday?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Descrivi una situazione complessa:

👉 solo con deduzioni

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • He must have been...
  • She might have been...

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 B1: descrivi 👉 B2: spieghi 👉 C1: interpreti

Advanced English is not about what you say. It is about how you think.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare il passive in modo naturale (non scolastico)
  • scegliere tra active e passive in base al contesto
  • usare il passive per essere più formale e professionale
  • costruire frasi più fluide e sofisticate

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • sai costruire il passive

👉 C1:

  • sai quando usarlo

👉 non è più:

✔️ “so fare il passive”

👉 ma:

✔️ “ha senso usarlo qui?”

docs.title: 🧩 Cos’è davvero il Passive?
docs.text:
👉 sposta il focus:

  • da chi fa l’azione → all’azione stessa

docs.title: ⚖️ Confronto
docs.text:
• Active: Someone wrote this report
  • Passive: This report was written

👉 nel passive:

❌ non importa chi ✔️ importa il risultato

docs.title: 🧠 Struttura base (rapida)
docs.text:
👉 be + past participle

Tempo Esempio Present is done Past was done Present Perfect has been done Future will be done

docs.title: 1. Quando NON interessa chi
docs.text:
• The report was finished
  • The project was completed

docs.title: 2. Quando è ovvio chi
docs.text: • The suspect was arrested 👉 (police)

docs.title: 3. Linguaggio formale
docs.text:
• The decision was made
  • The results were analysed

docs.title: Present Perfect Passive
docs.text: • The work has been completed

docs.title: Past Perfect Passive
docs.text: • The project had been finished before we arrived

docs.title: Future Passive
docs.text: • The work will be done tomorrow

docs.title: 🧩 Passive con modals
docs.text:
👉 struttura:

modal + be + past participle

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• The work must be done
  • This can be improved
  • The problem should be solved

docs.title: Active
docs.text:
• We finished the project

👉 più diretto

docs.title: Passive
docs.text:
• The project was finished

👉 più neutro / formale

docs.title: 🧠 Uso avanzato: Impersonal Passive
docs.text: 👉 molto usato in inglese formale

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• It is said that...
  • It is believed that...
  • It is known that...

👉 esempio:

It is believed that this method is effective

docs.title: 🧠 Passive con “get”
docs.text: 👉 più informale / parlato

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• He got fired
  • She got promoted

👉 differenza:

  • was fired → neutro
  • got fired → più personale

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: The project was completed ahead of schedule, and the results have been widely praised. It is believed that this approach will be used in future projects.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa passive per:

  • descrivere processi
  • parlare in modo formale

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: The product is developed in Italy and is exported worldwide.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (10–12 frasi):

👉 usa:

  • almeno 4 passive
  • almeno 2 tempi diversi

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto The work done The work was done The project is finish The project is finished It is say that It is said that

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 strutture chiave:

  • has been done
  • was completed
  • will be finished

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text: 👉 combina active + passive

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: We completed the project, and the results were published last week.

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia mentale
docs.text:
👉 chiediti:

  • importa chi fa l’azione?

👉 se NO → usa passive

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. The work ___ (finish) yesterday
  2. The report ___ (write) already
  3. The problem ___ (solve) soon
  4. The project must ___ (complete)

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. They finished the work → ______
  2. Someone wrote this → ______
  3. We will complete the project → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 descrivi:

  • un processo
  • un progetto
  • un prodotto

usa passive

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 3–4 minuti:

👉 usa almeno 5 passive

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • How is a product made?
  • How are decisions made in your company?
  • What is produced in your country?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Descrivi un processo completo:

👉 solo con passive

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • It is believed that...
  • It is expected that...

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 B1: usi active 👉 B2: conosci passive 👉 C1: scegli tra active e passive

Advanced English is about focus. Passive voice changes what matters.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • trasformare il discorso diretto in indiretto
  • usare reported speech in modo naturale
  • adattare tempi, pronomi e riferimenti
  • parlare e scrivere in modo più fluido e professionale

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • trasformi frasi

👉 C1:

  • racconti informazioni

👉 non stai più traducendo 👉 stai riportando idee

docs.title: Direct Speech
docs.text:
👉 parole esatte

  • He said: “I am tired”

docs.title: Reported Speech
docs.text:
👉 parole riportate

  • He said that he was tired

docs.title: 🔁 Backshifting (regola base)
docs.text:
👉 quando riporti nel passato:

Direct Reported present past past past perfect will would

docs.title: 📚 Esempi
docs.text:
• “I am tired” → He said he was tired
  • “I worked” → He said he had worked
  • “I will go” → He said he would go

docs.title: Pronomi
docs.text:
• I → he / she
  • my → his / her

docs.title: Tempo
docs.text:
• now → then
  • today → that day
  • yesterday → the day before
  • tomorrow → the next day

docs.title: Esempio completo
docs.text:
“I am working today”

👉 He said that he was working that day

docs.title: 🔥 Livello C1: Uso naturale
docs.text: 👉 NON sempre fai backshift

docs.title: Quando NON cambia
docs.text:
👉 se è ancora vero

  • “I live in Rome” → He said he lives in Rome

docs.title: 🧠 Reporting verbs (C1)
docs.text: 👉 non usare solo “say”

docs.title: Verbi comuni
docs.text: Verbo Uso say dire tell dire a qualcuno explain spiegare suggest suggerire admit ammettere promise promettere

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• He explained that he was late
  • She admitted that she had made a mistake
  • They promised they would help

docs.title: Yes / No questions
docs.text:
👉 if / whether

  • “Are you coming?” → He asked if I was coming

docs.title: Wh- questions
docs.text:
👉 mantieni la parola interrogativa

  • “Where do you live?” → He asked where I lived

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text: tell / ask + object + to + verb

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• “Close the door” → He told me to close the door
  • “Don’t be late” → He told me not to be late

docs.title: Suggest / recommend
docs.text:
👉 struttura:

suggest + verb-ing

  • He suggested going earlier

docs.title: Offer / promise
docs.text:
• He offered to help
  • She promised to call

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: She explained that she had been working on the project for months and that she was planning to finish it soon. She also mentioned that she might need some help.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa reported speech per raccontare:

  • conversazioni
  • riunioni
  • esperienze

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: He told me that the project was difficult and that we needed more time.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (10–12 frasi):

👉 racconta:

  • una conversazione
  • una riunione
  • qualcosa che qualcuno ha detto

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto He said me He told me He said me that He told me that He said that I am tired He said that I was tired

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 combinare più informazioni:

  • He said that he was tired and that he needed a break

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text: 👉 usa più reporting verbs:

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: He explained that the situation was complicated, and he suggested waiting before making a decision.

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia mentale
docs.text:
👉 non tradurre

👉 pensa:

  • cosa ha detto?
  • cosa è importante?

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. “I am tired” → He said he ___ tired
  2. “I will go” → He said he ___ go
  3. “Where do you live?” → He asked where I ___
  4. “Close the door” → He told me ___

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. “I am working” → ______
  2. “I went home” → ______
  3. “I will call you” → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 racconta:

  • una conversazione recente
  • cosa qualcuno ti ha detto

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 4 minuti:

👉 racconta una storia usando reported speech

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • What did your friend tell you recently?
  • What did your boss say?
  • What did you learn from others?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Racconta una conversazione complessa:

👉 usando almeno 5 reporting verbs

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • He mentioned that...
  • She pointed out that...

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 B1: ripeti 👉 B2: trasformi 👉 C1: racconti

Advanced English is not about repeating words. It is about transmitting meaning.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • collegare idee in modo avanzato
  • costruire discorsi chiari e complessi
  • usare connettori naturali e vari
  • migliorare writing e speaking in modo significativo

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • usa and, but, because

👉 C1:

  • usa una varietà di connessioni

👉 non è più:

✔️ frase → frase

👉 ma:

✔️ idea → sviluppo → contrasto → conclusione

docs.title: ➕ Addizione (aggiungere idee)
docs.text:
• moreover
  • furthermore
  • in addition

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• The course is useful. Moreover, it is practical.
  • In addition, it helps you improve speaking

docs.title: ⚖️ Contrasto
docs.text:
• however
  • although
  • even though
  • whereas

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• It is useful. However, it is difficult
  • Although it is difficult, I enjoy it

docs.title: 🔗 Causa ed effetto
docs.text:
• because
  • therefore
  • as a result
  • for this reason

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• I studied a lot, so I passed
  • I studied a lot; therefore, I passed

docs.title: 🎯 Risultato e conclusione
docs.text:
• in conclusion
  • overall
  • to sum up

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• In conclusion, this method is effective
  • Overall, it was a good experience

docs.title: 🧠 Livello C1: Varietà
docs.text:
👉 non ripetere sempre:

❌ because / and / but

👉 usa varietà:

✔️ however ✔️ therefore ✔️ although

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
❌ base:

  • I like it because it is useful and it is interesting

✔️ C1:

  • I like it because it is useful. Moreover, it is very interesting

docs.title: Struttura
docs.text:
1. idea
  2. sviluppo
  3. contrasto
  4. conclusione

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I think learning English is important because it gives you more opportunities. Moreover, it allows you to communicate with people from different cultures. However, it can be difficult at the beginning. In conclusion, it is worth the effort.

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: Learning English can open many doors. In addition, it allows you to travel more easily. However, it requires time and effort. For this reason, consistency is essential.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa:

  • moreover
  • however
  • therefore

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: It is useful. However, it takes time. Therefore, you need to be consistent.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi un testo (10–15 frasi):

👉 includi:

  • 3 tipi diversi di connectors
  • almeno 1 contrasto
  • almeno 1 conclusione

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto Because..., so... usa solo uno Although..., but... usa solo uno ripetere always "and" varia

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 strutture chiave:

  • Although..., ...
  • ..., however, ...
  • ..., therefore, ...

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)
docs.text: 👉 combinare più connettori

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: Although it is difficult, it is very useful. Moreover, it helps you grow. Therefore, I recommend it.

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia mentale
docs.text:
👉 pensa in blocchi:

  • idea
  • collegamento
  • sviluppo

docs.title: ✅ Review & Practice
docs.text:
Completa:

  1. It is useful. ___, it is difficult
  2. I studied a lot. ___, I passed
  3. ___ it is hard, I continue

docs.title: 🔄 Trasforma
docs.text:
1. It is good and useful → ______
  2. It is difficult but interesting → ______
  3. I studied so I passed → ______

docs.title: 🧠 Think & Speak
docs.text:
Parla:

👉 esprimi un’opinione completa

usa:

  • contrasto
  • causa
  • conclusione

docs.title: 🚀 Mini task
docs.text:
Parla per 4 minuti:

👉 usa almeno:

  • 3 connectors diversi

docs.title: 💬 Extra practice
docs.text:
Rispondi:

  • Why is English important?
  • What are the advantages and disadvantages?
  • What do you think about studying online?

docs.title: 🎯 Challenge (C1)
docs.text:
Crea un discorso completo:

👉 con almeno 5 connectors

docs.title: 🧩 Bonus (C1+)
docs.text:
Usa:

  • on the other hand
  • in contrast

docs.title: 🔥 Insight finale
docs.text:
👉 B1: frasi 👉 B2: paragrafi 👉 C1: discorsi

Advanced English is not about words. It is about how ideas are connected.

docs.title: 🎯 Obiettivo
docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • usare combinazioni di parole naturali
  • evitare errori tipici da traduzione
  • parlare e scrivere in modo più fluido
  • suonare più vicino a un madrelingua

docs.title: 🧠 Il salto da B2 a C1
docs.text:
👉 B2:

  • conosci parole

👉 C1:

  • conosci come si combinano

👉 non è:

✔️ parola giusta

👉 ma:

✔️ combinazione giusta

docs.title: 🧩 Cos’è una collocation?
docs.text: 👉 parole che “vanno insieme”

docs.title: Esempi
docs.text:
• make a decision ✔️
  • take a break ✔️
  • do homework ✔️

👉 NON sono casuali

docs.title: ❌ Errori tipici italiani
docs.text: Errore Corretto do a decision make a decision make a photo take a photo do a mistake make a mistake say me tell me

docs.title: 🔧 Verb + Noun
docs.text:
• make a decision
  • take responsibility
  • have experience

docs.title: 🔗 Adjective + Noun
docs.text:
• strong opinion
  • heavy rain
  • big mistake

docs.title: 🔄 Verb + Preposition
docs.text:
• depend on
  • focus on
  • deal with

docs.title: 💬 Expressions
docs.text:
• at the end of the day
  • in the long run
  • as far as I know

docs.title: 🔧 Verb + Noun
docs.text:
• reach a conclusion
  • draw attention
  • meet expectations
  • raise awareness

docs.title: 🔗 Adjective + Noun
docs.text:
• significant difference
  • major problem
  • key factor
  • strong argument

docs.title: 🔄 Verb + Object
docs.text:
• make progress
  • take action
  • give advice
  • reach a goal

docs.title: 🧠 Perché sono importanti?
docs.text:
👉 senza collocations:

❌ suoni “tradotto”

👉 con collocations:

✔️ suoni naturale

docs.title: 🔁 Confronto reale
docs.text:
❌ base:

  • I did a big decision

✔️ naturale:

  • I made an important decision

docs.title: 🧠 Strategia C1
docs.text:
👉 NON imparare parole isolate

👉 impara:

✔️ blocchi

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: ❌ decision ✔️ make a decision

docs.title: 🎧 Reading
docs.text: In order to make progress, it is important to take action and stay consistent. Many people fail because they don’t make the right decisions or they don’t follow through.

docs.title: 🗣️ Speaking
docs.text:
👉 usa collocations per:

  • opinioni
  • lavoro
  • esperienze

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text: I made a decision to change my job because I wanted to make progress.

docs.title: ✍️ Writing
docs.text:
Scrivi 10 frasi:

👉 usa almeno:

  • 5 collocations diverse

docs.title: ⚠️ Errori comuni (C1)
docs.text: Errore Corretto do progress make progress make homework do homework take a decision (UK vs US nuance) make a decision

docs.title: 🧠 Pattern avanzati
docs.text:
👉 blocchi utili:

  • make a decision
  • take action
  • reach a goal
  • give advice

docs.title: 🔁 Upgrade (C1 reale)

• look for → cercare

docs.text:
Alla fine di questa unità sarai in grado di:

  • scegliere la parola più precisa
  • capire differenze sottili tra parole simili

docs.text: 👉 piccola differenza di significato

docs.title: Esempio
docs.text:

👉 tutte corrette 👉 ma NON identiche

  • work → attività

  • issue → più formale / neutro
